paragraphs: This section deals with the impact from the green assets, as per the particular investments made in them. Any impact data is removed when the associated position is removed.

Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular position. The operation can return either a JSON formatted data set, or PDF file based on your template which you can provide to your customer as an investor report.
check Operation parameters
integer identifier for a investment
For decrypting personally identifiable information
Default is 0 decimals
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.
Input report template name
Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular investor. Here the impacts of multiple investments/positions are stacked on each other.
check Operation parameters
String identifier for an investor
For a specified asset of an investor
For decrypting personally identifiable information
Default is 0 decimals
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.
Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular portfolio. Here the impacts of multiple investments/positions are consolidated when possibel (when the green framworks are the same).
check Operation parameters

Use this operation to calculate the ESG impacts for a position without any data is stored or logged in ESG.Credits. This operation is applicable for cases when financial institutes wants to utilise ESG.Credits only for calculations and store and investor data in own data repositories.

pages.title: Investors | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents investor management operations including creating investors, generating encrypted identifiers, managing personally identifiable information (PII) with encryption keys, and retrieving investor data. Supports privacy-compliant investor record management for ESG portfolios.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new investor with encrypted PII information
Generates an encryption key for protecting personally identifiable information

useCases: Register green bond investors with privacy protection
Generate random investor identifiers to avoid PII exposure
Encrypt investor names and contact information securely
Manage multi-investor ESG portfolios with data compliance
Enable investor impact reporting with decryption capabilities

implementationSteps.title: Generate Encryption Key
implementationSteps.description: Use generateEncryptionKey to create a secure key for encrypting personally identifiable information

implementationSteps.title: Create Investor Identifier
implementationSteps.description: Call generateInvestorIdentifier to get a random identifier, or use your own PII-compliant identifier

implementationSteps.title: Register Investor
implementationSteps.description: Use addInvestor with encryption_key parameter to securely store investor information with encrypted PII

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits Solution Overview ESG Credits API Taxonomy Translations Currencies Frameworks Projects Issuers Assets Investors Positions Portfolios Impacts Blockchain Users X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions ESG Credits ESG Credits API Investors This section has operations to manage investors, i.e. showing, adding, encrypting, and removing investors getInvestors Returns all investors POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to retrieve all investors. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getInvestors parameters JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Copy [ { "investor_id" : 19 , "investor_identifier" : "3a6515809856" , "investor_name" : "John Smith" , "investor_currency_code" : "USD" , "investor_email" : " [email protected] " } , { "investor_id" : 20 , "investor_identifier" : "9c809eb86ee6" , "investor_name" : "Mary Smith" , "investor_currency_code" : "USD" , "investor_email" : " [email protected] " } ] Copy { "message" : "No investors are registered." } Copy { "message" : "Not authorized." } Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description depryption_key string no For decrypting personally identifiable information user_identifier string no For limiting the output to the investors that the user has access to Example code curl generateInvestorIdentifier Generates a random investor identifier POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to generate investor identifiers, for cases where they need to random and not personally identifiable information (PII). Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string generateInvestorIdentifier Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Example code curl generateEncryptionKey Generates an encryption key used to encrypt personal identifiable information POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to generate an encryption, used to encrypt and decrypt personally identiable infomation (PII) in the data repository of ESG.Credits. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string generateEncryptionKey Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Example code curl addInvestor Creates and updates the information of an existing investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to create investors stored in ESG.Credits. Is also used to alter already existing investor data. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addInvestor parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description investor_identifier string yes String identifier for an investor, potentially personally identifiable information (subject to encryption) investor_name string yes Personally identiable information (subject to encryption) investor_currency_code string yes Currency code of the investor, as per getCurrencies (currently not utilized) investor_email string no For investor correspondence e.g investor reports encryption_key string no For encrypting personally identifiable information Example code curl removeInvestor Removes an investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to remove investors from the ESG.Credits data repository. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Creditd operation * string removeInvestor parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description investor_identifier string yes String identifier for an investor Example code curl Previous Assets Next Positions Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? getInvestors Returns all investors generateInvestorIdentifier Generates a random investor identifier generateEncryptionKey Generates an encryption key used to encrypt personal identifiable information addInvestor Creates and updates the information of an existing investor removeInvestor Removes an investor Was this helpful? pages.title: Issuers | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents issuer management operations for green asset issuers (banks, corporations issuing green bonds). Covers adding, retrieving, and removing issuer information with public/private visibility settings. Essential for managing the supply side of green financing.

codeExamples.description: Adds a new green bond issuer with EUR currency and private visibility

useCases: Register banks issuing green bonds
Manage corporate issuers of sustainable financing instruments
Track green bond issuers across multiple markets
Configure issuer-specific currency settings
Control issuer visibility across BWS accounts

implementationSteps.title: Register Issuer
implementationSteps.description: Use addIssuer to create a new issuer record with identifier, name, currency, and visibility settings

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Issuer Data
implementationSteps.description: Call getIssuers with issuer_visibility parameter to list public or private issuers

implementationSteps.title: Update or Remove
implementationSteps.description: Use addIssuer to update existing issuer information or removeIssuer to delete issuer records (requires no associated assets)

Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getIssuers parameters JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error messagee Copy [ { "issuer_id" : 1 , "issuer_identifier" : "35f0c1a1c08" , "issuer_name" : "X Bank" , "issuer_currency_code" : "EUR" , "issuer_visibility" : "public" } , { "issuer_id" : 6 , "issuer_identifier" : "good-bank-issuer" , "issuer_name" : "Good Bank" , "issuer_currency_code" : "SEK" , "issuer_visibility" : "private" } ] Copy { "message" : "No such issuer is registered." } Copy { "message" : "Not authorized." } Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description issuer_visibility string no Public or private. Default is private, meaning not visible across different BWS accounts. Example code curl addIssuer Creates or updates information about an existing issuer POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to add issuers of green assets. This operation also alters already existing issuer information. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addIssuer parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description issuer_identifier string yes String identifier for an issuer issuer_name string yes Issuer name issuer_currency_code string yes Currency code of the asset, as per getCurrencies issuer_visibility string no Public or private. Default is private, meaning not visible across different BWS accounts. Example code curl removeIssuer Removes an issuer POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to remove issuers of green assets from the ESG.Credits data repository. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string removeIssuer parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description issuer_identifier string yes String identifier for an issuer Example code curl Previous Projects Next Assets Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? getIssuers Returns private or public issuers addIssuer Creates or updates information about an existing issuer removeIssuer Removes an issuer Was this helpful? Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.ESG.Credits", "operation": "getIssuers", "parameters": { "issuer_visibility": "public" } } Copy { "message": "Issuer was successfully added." } Copy { "message": "Invalid issuer visibility term used." } Copy { "message": "Not authorized." } Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.ESG.Credits", "operation": "addIssuer", "parameters": { "issuer_identifier": "good_issuer", "issuer_name": "Good Bank", "issuer_currency_code": "EUR", "issuer_visibility": "private" } } Copy { "Issuer was successfully removed." } Copy { "message": "Issuer was not removed. Asset(s) exist." } Copy { "message": "Not authorized." } Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.ESG.Credits", "operation": "removeIssuer", "parameters": { "issuer_identifier": "good_issuer" }

pages.title: Portfolios | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents portfolio management operations for organizing green asset investments. Covers creating portfolios, adding/removing positions, retrieving portfolio compositions, and managing investor-specific portfolio structures. Enables consolidated impact reporting at portfolio level.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new green portfolio for an investor
Adds an investment position to an investor's portfolio

useCases: Create diversified green bond portfolios for investors
Organize multiple green asset investments by investor
Calculate consolidated ESG impacts across portfolio holdings
Generate portfolio-level impact reports for investors
Manage multiple portfolios per investor (e.g., different strategies)

implementationSteps.title: Create Portfolio
implementationSteps.description: Use addPortfolio to create a new portfolio container linked to an investor identifier

implementationSteps.title: Add Positions
implementationSteps.description: Call addPositionToPortfolio to associate specific green asset investments with the portfolio

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Portfolio Data
implementationSteps.description: Use getPortfolios or getPortfolioPositions to view portfolio composition and asset holdings

Default is all. user_identifier string no For limiting the output to the investors that the user has access to decryption_key string no For decrypting personally identifiable information Example code curl getPortfolioPositions Returns all investments in green assets in a portfolio of an investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to retrieve all green investments in an investors portfolio. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getPortfolioPositions parameters * JSON check Operation Parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation Parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description portfolio_identifier string yes String identifier for a portfolio Example code curl addPortfolio Creates and updates the information of an existing portfolio of an investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to create a portfolio for an investor, next to be associated with specific investments. Impacts can then be consolidated on portfolio level. pages.title: Positions | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents position management operations for tracking individual green asset investments. Covers creating, updating, retrieving, and removing investment positions with automatic impact calculation. Links investors to specific green assets with investment amounts, currencies, and terms.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new investment position for an investor in a green asset

useCases: Record investor purchases of green bonds
Track investment amounts and terms for ESG impact calculation
Manage multi-currency green asset holdings
Link investors to specific sustainable financing instruments
Automatically calculate ESG impacts upon position creation

implementationSteps.title: Create Position
implementationSteps.description: Use addPosition to register a new investment linking an investor to a green asset with amount, currency, and term details

implementationSteps.title: Calculate Impact
implementationSteps.description: Impact is automatically calculated when reporting_year and position_days are provided in addPosition

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve or Remove
implementationSteps.description: Use getPositions to retrieve investor holdings or removePosition to delete investment records (also removes associated impacts)

pages.title: Projects | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents project management operations for tracking individual green projects (wind farms, solar installations, etc.). Covers creating projects, adding impact indicators, linking to frameworks, and consolidating multiple projects into frameworks. Supports project-level ESG benefit tracking.

codeExamples.description: Retrieves all public green projects for the 2023 reporting year

useCases: Register wind farm and solar projects with ESG benefits
Track allocated funding to individual green projects
Define project-specific environmental impact indicators
Consolidate multiple projects into a Green Bond Framework
Report project-level use of proceeds and impacts

implementationSteps.title: Create Project
implementationSteps.description: Use addProject to register a new green project with identifier, name, currency, and framework linkage

implementationSteps.title: Add Project Details
implementationSteps.description: Call addYearSpecificsToProject to set allocated amounts for the reporting year

implementationSteps.title: Define Impact Indicators
implementationSteps.description: Use addCategoryIndicatorToProject to add ESG benefit metrics (CO2 reduction, energy production, etc.)

implementationSteps.title: Build Framework
implementationSteps.description: Use buildFrameworkFromProjects to consolidate multiple projects into a unified green framework

Example code curl getProjectDetails Returns details about a green project for a particular reporting year POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operations to retrieve green project details. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getProjectDetails parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description project_identifier string yes String identifier for a project reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy language_code string no Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language. Example code curl addProject Creates and updates the information about a green project POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to add green projects to the ESG.Credits data repository. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addProject parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description project_identifier string yes String identifier for a project project_name string yes Project name project_currency_code string yes Currency code of the project, as per getCurrencies framework_identifier string yes String identifier for the framework to be built project_visibility string no Public or private. Default is private, meaning not visible across different BWS accounts. Example code curl addYearSpecificsToProject Adds reporting year specific details to a green project POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to set up the green project with details that are year specific. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addYearSpecificsToProject parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description project_identifier string yes String identifier for a project allocated_amount number/integer yes Amount of funding allocated to the project for the reporting year of the framework, for the corresponding impacts registered reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy Example code curl addCategoryIndicatorToProject Adds and updates category and indicator values to a green project POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to set up the different impact categories, indicators, and units, with specified values. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addCategoryIndicatorToProject parameters JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description project_identifier string yes String identifier for a project sub_category_id number/integer yes Integer identifier of a sub category sub_category_volume number yes Amount of funding / use of proceeds indicator_id number/integer yes Integer identifier of an indicator indicator_value number yes ESG benefit value, in the unit specified by the taxonomy reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy Example code curl buildFrameworkFromProjects Builds a green framework by consolidating benefit characteristics of projects POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to consolidate project impact data into a green framework, as an option to register the framework as a whole. The framework needs to be created and year specifics added. Projects need to refer to the framework. pages.title: Taxonomy | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents taxonomy management operations for building ESG classification systems. Covers retrieving UN SDGs, EU Environmental Objectives, and managing categories, sub-categories, indicators, and units. Enables custom taxonomy creation aligned with international sustainability standards.

codeExamples.description: Retrieves all UN Sustainable Development Goals in English

useCases: Build custom ESG taxonomies for green financing
Align impact categories with UN SDGs and EU Environmental Objectives
Define impact measurement units (CO2e kg, MWh, kW, etc.)
Create industry-specific sustainability indicators
Support multi-language taxonomy translations

implementationSteps.title: Explore SDGs and EU Objectives
implementationSteps.description: Use getSDGs and getEUObjectives to view available international sustainability frameworks

implementationSteps.title: Build Taxonomy Structure
implementationSteps.description: Use addCategory and addSubCategory to create your taxonomy hierarchy for the reporting year

implementationSteps.title: Define Indicators
implementationSteps.description: Add units with addUnit and link SDGs/EU objectives to sub-categories using addGoalObjectiveToSubCategory

Default is the original language. Example code curl getCategories Returns the categories of the taxonomy for a particular reporting year POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to retrieve all categories of your taxonomy. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getCategories parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy language_code string no Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language. Default is the original language. Example code curl getSuperIndicators Returns the super indicators of the taxonomy for a particular reporting year POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to retrieve all super indicators of your taxonomy. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getSuperIndicators parameters * JSON check Operation arameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description reporting_year number, 4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy language_code string no Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language. Example code curl getIndicators Returns the indicators and super indicators of the taxonomy for a particular reporting year POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to retrieve all indicators of your taxonomy. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getIndicators parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description reporting_year number, 4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy language_code string no Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language. Example code curl getUnits Returns the units of the taxonomy for a particular reporting year POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to retrieve all units of your taxonomy. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getUnits parameters * string check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description reporting_year number, 4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy language_code string no Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language. Example code curl addCategory Adds and updates a category to the taxonomy for a particular reporting year POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to add a category to your taxonomy. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addCategory parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description category_name string yes Category name to add or change reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy category_name_changed string no New category name language_code string yes Selection of original language code as per getLanguages Example code curl addSubCategory Adds and updates a sub category to the taxonomy for a particular reporting year POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to add a sub category to your taxonomy. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addSubCategory parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description sub_category_name string yes Sub category name to add or change category_id number/integer yes Integer identifier for a category reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy sub_category_name_changed string no New sub category name language_code string yes Selection of original language code as per getLanguages Example code curl addGoalObjectiveToSubCategory Adds a SDG and EU Objective to a sub category for a particular reporting year POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to add SDGs and EU Environmental Objectives to a sub category of your taxonomy. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addGoalObjectiveToSubCategory parameters * string check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description sub_category_id number, integer yes Integer identifier for a sub category reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy sdg_id number/integer no Integer identifier for an SDG eu_objective_id number/integer no Integer identifier for an EU Environmental Objective Example code curl addUnit Adds and updates a unit to the taxonomy for a particular reporting year POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to add a unit to your taxonomy. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addUnit parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description unit_name string yes Unit name to add or change reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy unit_name_changed string no New unit name language_code string yes Selection of original language code as per getLanguages Example code curl addSuperIndicator Adds and updates a super indicator to the taxonomy for a particular reporting year POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to add a super category to your taxonomy. pages.title: Translations | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Translations API for ESG Credits, enabling multilingual support for taxonomy components including categories, sub-categories, super-indicators, indicators, and units. Provides operations to retrieve available languages and add translations for different reporting years.

codeExamples.description: Retrieves all available language codes for registering translations in the ESG Credits taxonomy
Adds a Swedish translation for a specific category in the 2023 taxonomy
Adds a language translation to an indicator for a specific reporting year

useCases: Create multilingual ESG taxonomy frameworks for international reporting
Translate environmental impact categories into multiple languages
Localize ESG indicators for regional compliance requirements
Support multilingual investor reporting portals
Enable year-specific taxonomy translations for evolving standards

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Available Languages
implementationSteps.description: Call getLanguages operation to obtain all supported language codes (en, sv, dk, nb, fi, de, pl, fr, nl, es, pt, it)

implementationSteps.title: Add Taxonomy Translations
implementationSteps.description: Use addCategoryTranslation, addSubCategoryTranslation, addSuperIndicatorTranslation, and addIndicatorTranslation operations with category_id, reporting_year, language_code, and translated text

implementationSteps.title: Register Unit Translations
implementationSteps.description: Call addUnitTranslation for measurement units, providing unit_id, reporting_year, language_code, and translation (even if identical across languages)

pages.title: Users | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Users API for ESG Credits, providing operations to manage end users, user groups, and investor access permissions. Enables creation, retrieval, and deletion of users, as well as group assignments and investor access control for ESG reporting solutions.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new user with encrypted personal information and assigns a user role
Assigns a user to a specific group for access control purposes
Grants a user access to view specific investor information and portfolios

useCases: Manage user access to ESG investment portfolios and reports
Control which investors' data specific users can view
Organize users into groups (bankers, investors, capitalists, issuers, agents, exchanges, regulators)
Implement role-based access control for ESG reporting platforms
Encrypt sensitive user information with custom encryption keys

implementationSteps.title: Create Users
implementationSteps.description: Use addUser operation with user_identifier, name, email, role, and optional encryption_key for secure data storage

implementationSteps.title: Assign User Groups
implementationSteps.description: Call getGroups to retrieve available groups, then use addGroupToUser to assign users to appropriate groups for functionality access

implementationSteps.title: Configure Investor Access
implementationSteps.description: Use addInvestorToUser to link specific investors to users, controlling which investor portfolios and data each user can access

pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Provides an overview of the ESG Credits solution, a comprehensive API designed for financial institutions to add ESG impact reporting to digital channels. Supports impact calculations from green asset investments, customizable taxonomies, blockchain storage, and multi-currency reporting with ICMA framework compliance.
useCases: Banks providing investor ESG reports to retail customers for green asset portfolios
Institutional investors tracking and viewing impact reports for green asset investments
Venture capitalists offering sponsors consolidated impact reports across multiple projects
Issuers of green assets providing impact details to banks and advisors
Certification bodies digitally publishing quality-assured impact characteristics
Store ESG framework impact profiles immutably on blockchain for transparency
Issue blockchain certificates for customer investments in green assets
Prevent double-counting of green project benefits across debt capital markets

implementationSteps.title: Define Custom Taxonomy
implementationSteps.description: Build your taxonomy with two levels of impact categories and indicators, choosing ICMA framework or custom structure with UN SDGs and EU Environmental Objectives

implementationSteps.title: Configure Green Frameworks
implementationSteps.description: Set up green frameworks with impact profiles from green projects, supporting multiple currencies and languages for international reporting

implementationSteps.title: Generate Impact Reports
implementationSteps.description: Calculate impact amounts per annum or by instrument term, create investor reports, and optionally store profiles on blockchain for immutability

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits Solution Overview ESG Credits API X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions ESG Credits Solution Overview BWS.ESG.Credits solution overview This API solution is designed for financial institutions that want to quickly add ESG impact benefits to their digital channels and investment reporting. The benefits come from investments made in green assets. Typical use cases include: Banks and investment advisors who want to create and provide investor reports to their broad customer base in digital channels, for the specific scope of green assets they market. Institutional investors in green assets who want to create and view investment reports for their own investment portfolio of broadly available green assets. Venture capitalists funding green projects that want to create and provide their sponsors with investor reports on both project and consolidated levels, for each sponsor’s investment mix. Issuers of green assets that want to provide banks, advisors, and investors with impact details for promotional purposes, and for use in impact calculations. Certification bodies and agents that want to digitally publish quality-assured impact characteristics of green projects and green frameworks to issuers of green assets. By utilizing this API solution, your development team can accelerate the development of your ESG impact reporting solution. The underlying information model and calculation methods are designed to make it easy for you to add impact reporting to your digital channels and investment reporting. Impact reporting can be done as per the ICMA framework or any taxonomy of choice. The solution supports two levels of impact categories and two levels of impact indicators, as well as any impact unit. Impact amounts are calculated per annum or as per the term of the financial instrument. The green frameworks' “impact profile” forms the basis for calculations. Benefit characteristics of the green projects invested in can be consolidated and form your green framework. You can tie United Nations SDGs and EU’s Environmental Objectives classifications to taxonomy impact categories as per your choice. Any combination of currencies are supported, and your choice of taxonomy language can be accessed interchangeable. The ESG impact profile of your framework can be immutably stored in a blockchain to demonstrate your committment. Blockchain certificates for your customers' specific investment in green assets can also be issued with help of ESG.Credits. Finally, the blockchain can be utilized to ensure that green project benefits are not double-counted across the debt capital community. paragraphs: BWS.ESG.Credits solution overview

This API solution is designed for financial institutions that want to quickly add ESG impact benefits to their digital channels and investment reporting. The benefits come from investments made in green assets. Typical use cases include:
Banks and investment advisors who want to create and provide investor reports to their broad customer base in digital channels, for the specific scope of green assets they market.
Institutional investors in green assets who want to create and view investment reports for their own investment portfolio of broadly available green assets.
Venture capitalists funding green projects that want to create and provide their sponsors with investor reports on both project and consolidated levels, for each sponsor’s investment mix.
Certification bodies and agents that want to digitally publish quality-assured impact characteristics of green projects and green frameworks to issuers of green assets.

By utilizing this API solution, your development team can accelerate the development of your ESG impact reporting solution. The underlying information model and calculation methods are designed to make it easy for you to add impact reporting to your digital channels and investment reporting.

Impact reporting can be done as per the ICMA framework or any taxonomy of choice. The solution supports two levels of impact categories and two levels of impact indicators, as well as any impact unit.

Impact amounts are calculated per annum or as per the term of the financial instrument. The green frameworks' “impact profile” forms the basis for calculations. Benefit characteristics of the green projects invested in can be consolidated and form your green framework.

You can tie United Nations SDGs and EU’s Environmental Objectives classifications to taxonomy impact categories as per your choice. Any combination of currencies are supported, and your choice of taxonomy language can be accessed interchangeable.

The ESG impact profile of your framework can be immutably stored in a blockchain to demonstrate your committment. Blockchain certificates for your customers' specific investment in green assets can also be issued with help of ESG.Credits. Finally, the blockchain can be utilized to ensure that green project benefits are not double-counted across the debt capital community.

pages.title: FAN Game Cube | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Introduces FAN Game Cube as a B2B2C platform solution enabling sports clubs worldwide to create new revenue streams through NFT-based fan engagement. Allows fans to own virtual field sections and participate in multiple sports events including soccer, cricket, and chess tournaments.

pages.title: Calendar | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Calendar API for FAN Game Cube, representing scheduled games, matches, or tournaments. Provides operations to create, list, and delete calendar instances that anchor gameplay timelines for season-based or event-based fan campaigns.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new calendar instance for a season or tournament (e.g., LaLiga 2025-26)
Lists all calendars or retrieves a specific calendar by calendarId
Response when successfully creating a calendar, returns unique calendarId

useCases: Create calendar for UEFA Champions League Final single event
Schedule LaLiga 2025-2026 entire season with multiple matches
Manage tournament timelines for fan engagement campaigns
Link scheduled games to specific virtual fields
Track multiple concurrent sports events and competitions

implementationSteps.title: Create Calendar
implementationSteps.description: Call new_calendar operation with a name parameter (e.g., '2025-26' or 'UEFA Final') to create a time-bound event instance

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Calendar ID
implementationSteps.description: Store the returned calendarId from the API response to link with Field and Match objects

implementationSteps.title: Manage Calendars
implementationSteps.description: Use list_calendars to view all calendars or specific ones, and delete_calendar to remove calendars when no longer needed

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Cube API Calendar Represents a scheduled game, match, or tournament instance —essentially, a time-bound event associated with a specific Field. The Calendar object anchors the gameplay timeline, enabling season-based, game-based, or tournament-based fan campaigns. Examples: UEFA Champions League Final : Madrid vs PSG, for a single event calendar. LaLiga 2025-2026 , including multiple matches, for the entire season calendar. new_calendar POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_calendar parameters * JSON check method parameters new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption name string Calendar name (e.g. LaLiga 2025-26) new_calendar API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require ( 'axios' ); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution" : "BWS.NFT.GameCube" , "operation" : "new_calendar" , "parameters" : { "name" : "2025-26" } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method : 'post' , maxBodyLength : Infinity , url : 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' , headers : { 'X-Api-Key' : 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v' , /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type' : 'application/json' } , data : JSON .stringify (request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console .log ( JSON .stringify ( response .data)); }) .catch((error) => { console .log (error); }); new_calendar Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the calendarId , which will be used to link with related objects. list_calendars POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_fields parameters JSON (optional) check method parameters list_calendars Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption calendar_id string Calendar Id list_calendars API Call Example list_calendars API call using Node.js list_calendars Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the list of calendars you created or a single calendar if the CalendarId parameter is included in the call. delete_calendar POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_calendar parameters * JSON check method parameters delete_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption calendarId string Then Calendar Id of the calendar to delete. delete_calendar API Call Example delete_calendar API call using Node.js delete_calendar Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. pages.title: Cubes | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Cubes API for FAN Game Cube, representing zones or tiles within a Field. Each Cube is a unique NFT owned by fans, tracking ownership, enabling rewards, and reflecting live event impacts. Includes operations to list cubes and update cube prices.

codeExamples.description: Lists cubes for a specific field with pagination support using limit and lastEvaluatedKey
Updates the price for multiple cubes identified by their i_j coordinates to 5 USD
Response showing cube details including coordinates, dimensions, and unique identifiers

useCases: Tokenize sports field sections into NFT cubes for fan ownership
Track which fans own specific zones of virtual stadiums
Update pricing for premium field locations dynamically
Enable live event impacts to affect cube owner rewards
Manage hundreds of cubes per field with pagination

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Cubes
implementationSteps.description: Use list_cubes with fieldId and limit parameters to get cubes for a specific field, handling pagination with lastEvaluatedKey if more cubes exist

implementationSteps.title: Update Cube Pricing
implementationSteps.description: Call update_cube-price with fieldId, cubesList (using i_j coordinate format), and priceInCents to set USD pricing for specific cubes

implementationSteps.title: Track Ownership
implementationSteps.description: Monitor cube status and ownership data to enable rewards and live event impacts for cube owners during matches

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Cube API Cubes Represents a zone or tile within a Field . Each Cube is a unique NFT owned by a fan. It tracks ownership, enables rewards, and reflects live event impacts during matches. Cubes are generated automatically when creating a new field. list_cubes POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_cubes parameters * JSON check method parameters new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption fieldId string Field Id. limit number The maximum number of cubes returned (a field can have hundreds of cubes). lastEvaluatedKey (optional) If more cubes are available, include the returned key to get the next chink of cubes. list_cubes API Call Example list_cubes API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require ( 'axios' ); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution" : "BWS.NFT.GameCube" , "operation" : "list_cubes" , "parameters" : { "fieldId" : "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2" , "limit" : 5 } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method : 'post' , maxBodyLength : Infinity , url : 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' , headers : { 'X-Api-Key' : 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v' , /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type' : 'application/json' } , data : JSON .stringify (request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console .log ( JSON .stringify ( response .data)); }) .catch((error) => { console .log (error); }); list_cubes Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns a list of cubes and their related cube data, including the cubeId . If more cubes are available, the field lastEvaluatedKey is returned (include it on the API call for getting the next chunk of cubes). update_cube-price POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string update_cube-price parameters JSON check method parameters update_cube-price Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption field_id string Field Id cubesList list A list of cubes whose price needs to be updated. Use cube data i and j indexes to identify each cube using the format i_j (e.g. 40_0). priceInCents number Price in USD cents (e.g. 500 to indicate a 5USD cube price) update_cube-price API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js update_cube-price Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the number of correctly updated cubes, the number of invalid detected cubes, and the list of invalid cubes. pages.title: Event Types | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Event Types API for FAN Game Cube, defining in-game occurrences like goals, assists, or plays. Events are registered with coordinates and timestamps, affecting cube owners whose zones match the action location with assigned point values.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new event type 'pass' with 100 points assigned when detected on a cube
Updates an existing event type to assign 1000 points instead of the previous value
Response returning the unique eventTypeId for the newly created event type

useCases: Define scoring system for different in-game actions (goals, passes, assists)
Assign point values to specific event types for cube owner rewards
Create custom event types for different sports (football, chess, cricket)
Update event point values dynamically during seasons
Link event detection to cube locations for automated scoring

implementationSteps.title: Create Event Types
implementationSteps.description: Use new_event-type with fieldId, eventTypeName, and eventTypePoints to define game occurrences and their scoring values

implementationSteps.title: List and Manage Events
implementationSteps.description: Call list_event-types to retrieve all defined events for a field, and update_event-type to modify point values as needed

implementationSteps.title: Delete Unused Events
implementationSteps.description: Use delete_event-type with eventTypeId and fieldId to remove event types no longer needed for the game

pages.title: Field | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Field API for FAN Game Cube, representing virtual sports fields tokenized into interactive zones (CUBES). Each field is the foundational canvas for game interactions, typically linked to real-world sports venues with customizable dimensions and cube sizes.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new virtual field for Camp Nou stadium with 120m x 80m dimensions and 3m x 3m cubes
Retrieves field details for a specific fieldId or lists all created fields
Response containing the unique fieldId to link with cubes and calendar objects

useCases: Create virtual representations of Real Madrid Stadium
Tokenize Madison Square Garden into interactive fan zones
Set up custom field dimensions for different sports venues
Generate cubes automatically when creating new fields
Link physical sports venues to digital NFT experiences

implementationSteps.title: Define Field Parameters
implementationSteps.description: Specify field name, sport type, dimensions in centimeters (fieldLength, fieldWidth), and cube size (cubeLength) to create the virtual canvas

implementationSteps.title: Create Field
implementationSteps.description: Call new_field operation to create the field and automatically generate all cubes based on the specified dimensions

implementationSteps.title: Manage Fields
implementationSteps.description: Use list_fields to retrieve field details, and delete_field to remove fields along with all associated cubes when needed

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Cube API Field Represents a virtual sports field tokenized into interactive zones or sections ("CUBES"). It is the foundational canvas for all game interactions. Each field is typically linked to a real-world sports match. Examples: Real Madrid Stadium Madison Square Garden new_field POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_field parameters * JSON check method parameters new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption name string Field name (e.g. Camp Nou) sport string Sport (e.g. Football) fieldLength number Field length in centimeters (e.g. 12000) fieldWidth number Field width in centimeters (e.g. 9000) cubeLength number The square size of a single cube (e.g. 300 for a cube of 3x3 meters) new_field API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js new_field Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the fieldId , which will be used to link NFT Game Cube related objects. All the cubes for that field are created when creating a new field. list_fields POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_fields parameters JSON (optional) check method parameters list_fields Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption field_id string Field Id list_fields API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js list_fields Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the list of fields you created or a single field if fieldId parameter is included in the call. delete_field POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_field parameters * JSON check method parameters delete_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption fieldId string Field Id to delete. delete_field API Call Example delete_field API call using Node.js delete_field Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. All the cubes created and assigned to the deleted field are also deleted. pages.title: Live Events | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Live Events API for FAN Game Cube, recording in-match events at specific coordinates during scheduled matches. Serves as the primary mechanism for capturing game data like goals, moves, and fouls, mapping them to corresponding cubes for scoring and rewards.

codeExamples.description: Registers a live event at coordinates (45, 40) on the field during a match for scoring
Successful response after recording the live event

useCases: Record goals and assists with coordinate data during live matches
Capture chess moves and map them to board positions
Automatically detect game events using AI/ML models
Manually log events through organizer dashboard interface
Integrate real-time event feeds from sports data providers
Calculate cube owner scores based on event locations
Process rewards for fans whose cubes match event coordinates

implementationSteps.title: Prepare Event Data
implementationSteps.description: Gather matchId, fieldId, calendarId, event coordinates (x, y), and eventTypeId for the occurrence you want to record

implementationSteps.title: Submit Live Event
implementationSteps.description: Call new_event operation with all required parameters to register the event and trigger scoring calculations for affected cubes

implementationSteps.title: Integrate Event Sources
implementationSteps.description: Connect manual input dashboards, AI/ML detection models, or application APIs to automatically push live events during matches

It is the primary mechanism by which game data—such as goals, moves, fouls, or plays—is captured and mapped to corresponding Cubes for scoring and reward processing. This API serves as a trusted interface to power fan engagement: Manually via the organizer dashboard or solution UI (BWS Solution Interface) Automatically via BWS-provided machine learning models that detect and push game events in real time (e.g., AI-driven goal recognition, chess move capture) Through your application. new_event POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_event parameters * JSON check method parameters new_event Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption matchId string The Match Id for which you want to register the event. fieldId string The Field Id the match is linked to. calendarId string The calendar Id the match is linked to. event JSON check event parameter new_event Event Parameter Parameter Type Desciption coordinates JSON check coordinates parameter eventTypeId string The Event Type Id of the event you want to register. new_event Coordinates Parameter Parameter Type Desciption x string The 2D x coordinate of the event. y string The 2D y coordinate of the event. new_event API Call Example new_event API call using Node.js new_event Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. pages.title: Match | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for managing Match objects in FAN Game Cube. A Match represents a time-bound gameplay session tied to a specific Field where events are tracked, points awarded, and fan interactions occur. Includes operations to create, update, list, and delete matches with team information, images, and pricing.

codeExamples.description: Create a new match for PSG vs Manchester City with scheduled start time and team details
Update match description for an existing match
List matches for a calendar or retrieve specific match details

useCases: Create football matches like PSG vs Manchester City with team flags and descriptions
Schedule chess tournament rounds between players like Magnus Carlsen vs Hikaru Nakamura
Manage basketball games between teams like Green Bay Packers vs Chicago Bears
Update match status to scheduled, playing, finished, or canceled
Set pricing for cube purchases during specific matches

implementationSteps.title: Create New Match
implementationSteps.description: Call new_match operation with calendarId, startTimeInMillis, team names, flags (base64 encoded images), and optional pricing in USD cents

implementationSteps.title: Update Match Details
implementationSteps.description: Use update_match with matchId and calendarId to modify match properties like description, status, team information, or pricing

implementationSteps.title: Manage Match Lifecycle
implementationSteps.description: List matches to retrieve details, delete matches when needed, and track match status throughout the game lifecycle

Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Cube API Match A Match is a time-bound, gameplay session tied to a specific Field, during which events are tracked, points are awarded, and fan interactions occur. It represents a game instance—such as a football match, chess round, or tournament heat—scheduled via the Calendar object and hosted on a defined Field .\ Examples: PSG vs. Manchester City Green Bay Packers vs Chicago Bears Magnus Carlsen vs Hikaru Nakamura Images The images sent using base64 encoding are saved into IPFS using the BWS IPFS Solution. When getting object details, the IPFS URL is returned. new_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_match parameters * JSON check method parameters new_match Method Parameters Optionals All parameters are optional (except calendarId and startTimeInMillis). Parameter Type Desciption calendarId * string Calendar Id the match should be included in. startTimeInMillis * number The expected match start time in milliseconds. name string The match short name. description string Match description. image string An image base64 encoded string to announce the match. team1Name string The team name (e.g. Manchester City) team1Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. team2Name string The team name (e.g. LA Lakers) team2Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. status string The match status. Use one of the following values: "scheduled", "playing", "finished", "canceled". priceInCents number The default price in USD cents for all the cubes if no specific price is defined for a cube (e.g. 1000 for 10 USD). new_match API Call Example new_match API call using Node.js new_match Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matchId for the newly created match. update_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string update_match parameters * JSON check method parameters update_match Method Parameters Optionals All parameters are optional (except calendarId and matchId) Only provide those you want to update. Parameter Type Desciption calendarId * string Calendar Id the match is included in. matchId * string The Match Id we want to update. startTimeInMillis number The expected match start time in milliseconds. name string The match short name. description string Match description. image string An image base64 encoded string to announce the match. team1Name string The team name (e.g. Manchester City) team1Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. team2Name string The team name (e.g. LA Lakers) team2Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. status string The match status. Use one of the following values: "scheduled", "playing", "finished", "canceled" priceInCents number The price in USD cents (e.g. 1000 for 10 USD) update_match API Call Example update_match API call using Node.js update_match Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matchId for the updated match. list_matches POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_matches parameters JSON check method parameters list_matches Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption calendar_id string The Calendar Id the match is linked to. matchId string (optional) The Match Id we want to list. list_matches API Call Example list_matches API call using Node.js list_matches Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matches for the provided calendar. If we give a Match Id, it will just return the match we want to list. delete_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_calendar parameters * JSON check method parameters delete_match Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption matchId string The Match Id of the match we want to delete. calendarId string The Calendar Id the match is linked to. delete_match API Call Example delete_match API call using Node.js delete_match Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. Previous Plays (Field-Calendar) Next Prizes Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? new_match Request Body new_match API Call Example update_match Request Body update_match API Call Example list_matches Request Body list_matches API Call Example delete_match Request Body delete_match API Call Example Was this helpful? pages.title: Plays (Field-Calendar) | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for managing Field-Calendar relationships in NFT Game Cube. Models the temporal lifecycle of a Field by linking static spatial configurations (like a 12×8 football pitch) with dynamic Calendar entries representing individual matches, tournaments, or rounds. Images are automatically stored on IPFS via BWS IPFS Solution.

codeExamples.description: Create a new field-calendar relationship for PSG 2025-26 season with image uploaded to IPFS
List all calendars associated with a specific field
Response showing field-calendar with IPFS URL for stored image

useCases: Link PSG Champions League 2026 calendar to a football field configuration
Create New York Knicks 2026 NBA Season calendar for basketball court
Manage multiple tournament seasons on the same sports field
Track temporal progression of matches and events on static field layouts
Store season promotional images on IPFS for permanent accessibility

implementationSteps.title: Create Field-Calendar Relationship
implementationSteps.description: Call new_field-calendar with fieldId, calendarId, description (e.g., 'PSG 2025-26'), and optional base64 encoded image that will be stored on IPFS

implementationSteps.title: List Field Calendars
implementationSteps.description: Use list_field-calendars with fieldId to retrieve all calendars associated with a specific field, including IPFS URLs for images

implementationSteps.title: Delete Relationships
implementationSteps.description: Remove field-calendar associations using delete_field-calendar with both fieldId and calendarId when seasons or tournaments end

pages.title: Prizes | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for managing Prize objects in NFT Game Cube. Prizes represent reward assets or benefits users receive based on CUBE performance in specific matches or seasons. Can include digital NFTs, token bonuses, physical goods, or experiences like signed merchandise, VIP tickets, or athlete meet-and-greets.

codeExamples.description: Create a first place prize for a match with signed merchandise as reward
List all prizes configured for a specific match with IPFS image URLs
Update prize details including HTML description for rich formatting

useCases: Award signed merchandise like framed jerseys or equipment to top performers
Offer VIP match tickets to first, second, and third place winners
Provide Zoom calls or AMA sessions with athletes as exclusive prizes
Distribute bonus BWS tokens to cube owners based on performance
Create digital NFT rewards for tournament winners

implementationSteps.title: Create Match Prize
implementationSteps.description: Call new_match-prize with matchId, calendarId, position (1-3), prize name, description, and snapshot image (base64 encoded)

implementationSteps.title: Configure Prize Details
implementationSteps.description: Add optional htmlDescription for rich formatting, and up to 3 additional images (image1, image2, image3) to showcase the prize

implementationSteps.title: Manage Prize Lifecycle
implementationSteps.description: Update prize details as needed, list prizes for matches to display to fans, or delete prizes when matches are canceled or prizes change

pages.title: FAN Game Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Comprehensive overview of FAN Game Cube platform that revolutionizes sports fan engagement through NFT-based field ownership. Explains how fans purchase digital cubes representing sections of sports fields, earn points from real-time events, and unlock rewards while creating new revenue streams for sports organizations across multiple sports including football, chess, tennis, cricket, basketball, and more.
useCases: Football stadiums divided into 500-2000 cubes for match-based fan engagement
Chess tournament tokenization of 64 chessboard squares for interactive viewing
Tennis courts with tokenized baseline, service boxes, and net areas
Cricket field division into batting zones, fielding areas, and boundary sections
Basketball court cube allocation including three-point arc, free-throw line, and paint zone
Launch 500 cubes at $500 average to generate $250,000 revenue for VIP events

implementationSteps.title: Configure Sport-Specific Cubes
implementationSteps.description: Define cube allocation based on sport characteristics - football fields with 500-2000 cubes focusing on penalty boxes and midfield, chess with 64 board squares, basketball with key court zones

implementationSteps.title: Integrate Purchase Widget
implementationSteps.description: Embed one-click purchase widget on club website for frictionless transactions using credit cards, allowing fans to buy cubes as easily as game tickets

implementationSteps.title: Enable Real-Time Engagement
implementationSteps.description: Provide fans with mobile app access to manage cube collections, track live matches, join leaderboards, participate in live-streaming, and engage in interactive chat

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview Building New Revenue Streams for the Sports Industry BWS's FAN Game Cube platform revolutionizes fan engagement by allowing sports enthusiasts to own digital assets linked to specific sections of a sports field. This extends beyond football to include chess, tennis, cricket, basketball, rugby, baseball, hockey, and any sport with a field that can be split into "cubes". As real-time events unfold in their selected area, fans earn points and unlock exciting rewards, all while creating new revenue opportunities for sports clubs, former athletes, influencers, and other key figures in the sports industry. Want to launch a FAN Game Cube event? Contact us here . Cube Allocation for Different Sports Sports organizers can define how many cubes to sell and how to divide their playing area based on the sport’s unique characteristics. A football/soccer field cubes division. Examples include: Football stadiums It is typically divided into 500, 1000, or 2000 cubes , ensuring key areas like penalty boxes and midfield zones hold premium value. Chess tournaments The 64 chessboard squares can be tokenized, offering fans a unique way to interact with their favorite chess events. Tennis courts Tokenizing the baseline, service boxes, and net area allows fans to engage in real-time match events. Cricket fields Dividing the pitch into batting zones, fielding areas, and boundary sections enhances fan participation. Basketball courts Cube allocation can include key areas like the three-point arc, free-throw line, and paint zone . Rugby fields Fans can purchase cubes covering try zones, scrums, and midfield sections . Baseball fields Key locations such as bases, outfield zones, and pitcher’s mound can be tokenized. And a long etcetera. NFT Technology for Ownership and Trading FAN Game Cube utilizes NFT technology to ensure verifiable ownership. However, the usage of NFT goes beyond ownership, allowing fans to trade their cubes as they would with traditional assets. During a season or tournament, cube owners can buy and sell cubes mid-season on secondary marketplaces, generating additional royalties for sports clubs and organizers. Since NFTs offer permanent and transparent ownership records , fans gain long-term value while clubs benefit from secondary sales. Pricing and Monetization Strategy Organizers have complete control over pricing, ensuring dynamic valuation based on strategic cube placement. Different pricing strategies include: Prime field areas Higher prices can be charged for high-demand sections, such as the center of a football pitch, penalty spots, or key cricket fielding positions. Secondary zones To encourage broad participation, less strategic locations, such as side areas or backfield positions, can be offered at $10 or lower. Dynamic pricing models Pricing can fluctuate based on match importance, demand, and historical engagement levels. Revenue Potential Your expected revenue is calculated as follows: Number of cubes × Average Price per cube = Total income For example, launching 500 cubes at an average price of $500 per cube on a VIP event can generate $250,000 in revenue. Larger stadiums, premium tournaments, or full-season FAN Game Cubes can scale this model to generate even higher revenues. Seamless Integration with One-Click Purchase Widget BWS provides a one-click purchase widget for frictionless transactions. Sports clubs do not need to create special accounts or navigate complex payment gateways—cubes can be sold in just a few hours. A weekend Chess Tournament NFT Game Cube event. The widget integrates easily into existing club websites, allowing fans to purchase cubes as simply as buying a regular game ticket. How Fans Can Participate and Win Once a sports club publishes its one-click buy widget , fans can easily purchase FAN Game Cubes using standard credit cards, making participation straightforward and accessible. One-click FAN Game Cube purchase experience This experience brings a new layer of engagement to the fan experience while giving them direct ownership of in-game digital assets. Real-Time Fan Engagement and Play & Win Mechanics NFT-based cubes offer fans an engaging way to experience their favorite sports, providing: True ownership of digital sports assets. Fans can buy, sell, or hold tradeable assets for future value (full-season FAN Game Cube). Passive earnings potential through resale royalties and long-term value accumulation. Access to real-time engagement through a dedicated mobile app. Once a cube is purchased, fans can: Manage their cube collection across multiple sports. Track upcoming matches in real time. Join live leaderboards to compete with other fans for top prizes. Why Choose FAN Game Cube? Multi-Sport Scalability Works across various sports, including football, cricket, chess, basketball, rugby, baseball, hockey, golf, Formula 1, and esports. It is flexible and adaptable to different event structures and fan bases. One-Click Buy Widget A JavaScript widget for effortless integration that enhances the club’s purchasing experience with quick, seamless transactions. Easy & Free to Launch Clubs and organizers can set up Fan Game Cube events using a simple three-step workflow or API calls. Flexible Event Calendars Supports single VIP games, full-season matches, and tournaments. It is scalable, from small local events like the Ohio Chess Tournament to major leagues like NBA teams. Real-Time Fan Engagement A comprehensive mobile app enables clubs to foster deep connections with fans during matches. Features include live-streaming for real-time game viewing and interactive chat to enhance fan engagement and discussion. BWS Fan Game Cube is the future of sports engagement, bridging the digital and real-world experiences for fans while unlocking new revenue opportunities for sports organizations. Get started today and transform the way fans interact with their favorite games! Previous FAN Game Cube Next FAN Game Cube API Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? Cube Allocation for Different Sports NFT Technology for Ownership and Trading Pricing and Monetization Strategy Seamless Integration with One-Click Purchase Widget How Fans Can Participate and Win Real-Time Fan Engagement and Play & Win Mechanics Why Choose FAN Game Cube?
