Commands: /list_filters - Lists all configured filters for the current group, showing their names and a summary of what each. /show_filter - Displays the complete configuration of a filter including all tracked accounts, keywords, cashtags,. /add_accounts - Adds one or more X (Twitter) accounts to track. /add_keywords - Adds keywords or phrases to track in X posts. /add_cashtags - Adds cashtags (token symbols) to track in X posts. /add_mentions - Adds X handles to track when they are mentioned in posts. /add_excludes - Adds keywords or phrases to exclude from tracking. /add_ignore - Adds X accounts to ignore. /remove_accounts - Removes specific X accounts from tracking. /remove_keywords - Removes specific keywords or phrases from tracking. /remove_cashtags - Removes specific cashtags from tracking. /remove_mentions - Removes X handles from mention tracking. /remove_excludes - Removes keywords from the exclusion list. /remove_ignore - Removes X accounts from the ignore list. /clear_accounts - Removes ALL tracked accounts from the filter. /clear_keywords - Removes ALL keywords from the filter. /clear_cashtags - Removes ALL cashtags from the filter. /clear_mentions - Removes ALL mentions from the filter. /clear_excludes - Removes ALL excluded keywords from the filter. /clear_ignore - Removes ALL ignored accounts from the filter. Basic Commands (4 commands) Essential bot operations including help, initialization, and status checks. Commands: /help - Displays a complete list of available bot commands and their descriptions. /start - Initializes the bot and provides welcome messages. /status - Checks if the bot is operational and running properly in your group. /get_chatid - Displays your Telegram group's unique chat ID. Advanced Filtering (3 commands) Create named filters for complex tracking scenarios and multi-project management. Commands: /set_x_filtering - Creates advanced named filters with complex X API query syntax. /get_x_filtering - Lists all advanced named filters configured for your group. /delete_x_filtering - Deletes advanced named filters created with /set_x_filtering. Project Configuration (10 commands) Customize project metadata including name, description, logo, and URLs for reports. Commands: /set_project_name - Sets your project or community name that will appear in all generated reports, leaderboards, and on the. /get_project_name - Displays the current project name configured for your community. /set_project_description - Sets a short description of your project that appears in reports and on the website. /get_project_description - Displays the current project description configured for your community. /set_project_long_description - Sets a detailed, comprehensive description of your project for the website dashboard. /get_project_long_description - Displays the current detailed project description configured for the website dashboard. /set_project_logo - Uploads your project logo that will appear on reports and the website dashboard. /get_project_logo - Displays the URL of your currently configured project logo. /set_project_urls - Sets important links for your project including website, social media, documentation, and other resources. /get_project_urls - Displays all project URLs currently configured for your community. Raid Commands (11 commands) Coordinate X (Twitter) engagement raids to amplify post visibility and reach. Commands: /raidx - Launches a coordinated community raid on an X (Twitter) post to boost engagement. /raid_status - Displays real-time progress of active raids, showing current engagement numbers vs. /raid_stop - Cancels an active raid immediately, stopping progress tracking and notifications. /raid_history - Displays a list of previous raids with their completion status, targets, and results. /set_raid_defaults - Configures default settings for all future raids including target engagement numbers, duration, auto-mute behavior,. /get_raid_defaults - Displays the current default raid settings including target numbers for all engagement types, default duration,. /set_raid_message_behavior - Controls how the bot updates raid progress messages in the chat. /get_raid_message_behavior - Shows the currently configured raid message update mode (edit, repost, or pin) and explains what that mode does. /set_raid_image - Uploads a custom image that appears in all raid announcement messages for your community. /auto_raid_config - Configures automatic raids that trigger for the "Best Tweet of the Day" selected during daily report generation. /get_auto_raid_config - Displays the current auto-raid configuration including whether it's enabled, the raid duration, start delay after. Schedule Management (3 commands) Configure automated report generation schedules with custom intervals. Commands: /set_schedule - Sets a schedule for when reports should be automatically generated. /get_schedule - Retrieves and displays the current scheduled time for automatic report generation. /delete_schedule - Removes the configured daily schedule for automatic report generation. Calendar & Time (2 commands) Manage calendar settings and time periods for report generation cycles. Commands: /set_calendar - Sets up a recurring calendar schedule for automatic report generation. /get_calendar - Displays the current calendar schedule configuration for automatic reports, showing the start date and how often. Reports (2 commands) Generate and manage performance analytics reports for tracked X content. Commands: /report - Displays the most recently generated leaderboard report for your community. /recreate - Triggers immediate generation of a fresh report by fetching latest X (Twitter) data and creating new leaderboards. Leaderboard Customization (10 commands) Customize scoring points, colors, titles, and display settings for leaderboard reports. Commands: /set_points - Sets the scoring multipliers for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views). /get_points - Retrieves and displays the current scoring multipliers for engagement metrics. /set_colors - Sets the HTML colors for displaying engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views). /get_colors - Retrieves and displays the current HTML color configuration for engagement metrics. /set_top_title - Sets the title text and color for the main leaderboard section in generated reports. /set_best_title - Sets the title text and color for the best tweet section in generated reports. /set_engagement_title - Sets the title text and color for the engagement scoring section in generated reports. /get_titles - Retrieves and displays all current title configurations for the report sections including. /set_top_count - Sets the number of users to display in the report leaderboard. /get_top_count - Retrieves and displays the current number of users configured to show in the report. Topic Management (2 commands) Configure Telegram topic/thread settings for bot operation. Commands: /set_topic - Configures which forum topic (thread) the bot should send reports to in Telegram groups that use forum-style topics. /get_topic - Displays which forum topic (thread) is currently configured to receive bot reports. Admin (1 commands) Administrative functions for managing bot notifications and permissions. Commands: /add_admin - Designates specific group administrators to receive important bot notifications via private messages, such as. Subscription & Credits (5 commands) Manage billing, subscriptions, credits, and usage tracking. Commands: /subscription - Provides access to Stripe customer portal for subscription management including billing history,. /buy - Initiates the purchase process for X Bot Professional subscription. /get_credits - Displays the current credit usage and availability for the bot. /payment_success - Handles successful payment confirmation after Stripe checkout completion. /payment_cancelled - Handles payment cancellation when users exit Stripe checkout without completing payment. pages.title: Admin | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documentation for the /add_admin command that designates specific group administrators to receive important bot notifications via private messages. Helps reduce notification noise by limiting alerts about credit exhaustion, subscription renewals, and errors to admins who manage the bot. All designated users must already be group administrators.

codeExamples.description: Clear all designated admins to reset notification recipients
Designate specific users to receive bot notifications

useCases: Designate specific admins to receive credit exhaustion warnings
Limit subscription renewal notifications to billing managers
Send error alerts only to technical administrators
Clear admin list to reset notification recipients to all group admins

pages.title: Advanced Filtering | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documentation for three advanced filtering commands that create named filters with complex X API query syntax. Supports combining multiple criteria using logical operators (OR, AND) and special operators (from:, keywords:, mention:, exclude:, ignore:) for sophisticated tracking scenarios beyond simple add/remove commands. Admin-only operations for managing complex multi-project tracking.

codeExamples.description: Create named filter to track mentions of project handle and hashtag
Track tweets from specific KOL accounts containing BWS keyword
Monitor trending crypto hashtags while excluding spam content

useCases: Track multiple project mentions across different hashtags and handles
Monitor tweets from key opinion leaders mentioning specific keywords
Create complex filters combining hashtags with exclusions and ignore lists
Manage multiple named filters for different tracking scenarios
Review and delete sophisticated filtering setups

implementationSteps.title: Create Named Filter
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_x_filtering with filter name and complex query syntax combining from:, keywords:, mention:, exclude:, and ignore: operators with OR/AND logic

implementationSteps.title: Review Filters
implementationSteps.description: Run /get_x_filtering to list all advanced named filters with their complete X API query syntax

implementationSteps.title: Delete Filters
implementationSteps.description: Remove specific named filters with /delete_x_filtering or use wildcard '*' to delete all advanced filters at once

pages.title: Filter Setup | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Comprehensive reference documentation for X Bot filter setup commands. Covers viewing, adding, removing, and clearing filters for tracking X (Twitter) accounts, keywords, cashtags, mentions, exclusions, and ignored accounts. All commands are admin-only and enable precise control over social media monitoring.

codeExamples.description: Add multiple X (Twitter) accounts to track their posts for monitoring and reporting
Add keywords and phrases (with quotes for multi-word terms) to track in X posts
Add cryptocurrency cashtags to a named filter for token-specific tracking

useCases: Track crypto project team members and key opinion leaders (KOLs)
Monitor brand mentions and hashtag campaigns across Twitter
Filter cryptocurrency token discussions by cashtag symbols
Exclude spam and bot accounts from engagement analytics

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions X Bot X Bot Commands Filter Setup View Filters /list_filters Lists all configured filters for the current group, showing their names and a summary of what each filter tracks. Useful for managing multiple filters and understanding your current tracking setup. Admin Only Copy /list_filters /show_filter Displays the complete configuration of a filter including all tracked accounts, keywords, cashtags, mentions, exclusions, and ignored accounts. Shows the resulting X API query that will be used for tracking. Admin Only Copy /show_filter Copy /show_filter filter=kols Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional kols Add Filters /add_accounts Adds one or more X (Twitter) accounts to track. Posts from these accounts will be monitored and included in reports and leaderboards. You can optionally specify a named filter to organize different account groups (e.g., team members, influencers, competitors). Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example accounts required @CryptoHayes filter optional team /add_keywords Adds keywords or phrases to track in X posts. Any post containing these keywords will be monitored and included in reports. Use quotes for multi-word phrases. Keywords can include hashtags. Helps filter content by topics, brands, or specific terms relevant to your project. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example keywords required crypto filter optional trending /add_cashtags Adds cashtags (token symbols) to track in X posts. Any post containing these cashtags will be monitored and included in reports. Cashtags must start with $ symbol. Useful for tracking cryptocurrency tokens, stock symbols, or project-specific ticker symbols. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example cashtags required $BWS filter optional tokens /add_mentions Adds X handles to track when they are mentioned in posts. Any post that mentions these accounts (using @handle) will be monitored and included in reports. Different from add_accounts which tracks posts FROM accounts - this tracks posts ABOUT/MENTIONING accounts. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example mentions required value filter optional influencers /add_excludes Adds keywords or phrases to exclude from tracking. Posts containing these excluded terms will be filtered out and NOT included in reports, even if they match other filter criteria. Use this to remove noise, spam, or irrelevant content from your tracking. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example keywords required spam filter optional clean /add_ignore Adds X accounts to ignore. Posts from these accounts will be filtered out and NOT included in reports, even if they match other filter criteria. Use this to exclude bot accounts, spam accounts, or specific users whose posts you don't want to track. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example accounts required @SpamBot filter optional clean Remove/Clear Filters /remove_accounts Removes specific X accounts from tracking. Posts from these accounts will no longer be monitored or included in reports. Use this to stop tracking accounts that are no longer relevant to your project. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example accounts required @OldAccount filter optional kols /remove_keywords Removes specific keywords or phrases from tracking. Posts containing only these keywords will no longer be monitored. Use quotes for multi-word phrases. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example keywords required oldterm filter optional trending /remove_cashtags Removes specific cashtags from tracking. Posts containing only these cashtags will no longer be monitored. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example cashtags required $OLDTOKEN filter optional tokens /remove_mentions Removes X handles from mention tracking. Posts mentioning only these accounts will no longer be monitored. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example mentions required value filter optional influencers /remove_excludes Removes keywords from the exclusion list. Posts containing these terms will no longer be automatically filtered out. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example keywords required spam filter optional clean /remove_ignore Removes X accounts from the ignore list. Posts from these accounts will now be tracked if they match filter criteria. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example accounts required @ReinstatedAccount filter optional clean /clear_accounts Removes ALL tracked accounts from the filter. Use this to reset account tracking and start fresh. This does not delete the filter itself, only clears the accounts list. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional kols /clear_keywords Removes ALL keywords from the filter. Use this to reset keyword tracking. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional trending /clear_cashtags Removes ALL cashtags from the filter. Use this to reset cashtag tracking. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional tokens /clear_mentions Removes ALL mentions from the filter. Use this to reset mention tracking. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional influencers /clear_excludes Removes ALL excluded keywords from the filter. Posts with previously excluded terms will now be tracked. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional clean /clear_ignore Removes ALL ignored accounts from the filter. Posts from previously ignored accounts will now be tracked. paragraphs: Lists all configured filters for the current group, showing their names and a summary of what each filter tracks. Useful for managing multiple filters and understanding your current tracking setup.

Displays the complete configuration of a filter including all tracked accounts, keywords, cashtags, mentions, exclusions, and ignored accounts. Shows the resulting X API query that will be used for tracking.

Adds one or more X (Twitter) accounts to track. Posts from these accounts will be monitored and included in reports and leaderboards. You can optionally specify a named filter to organize different account groups (e.g., team members, influencers, competitors).

Adds keywords or phrases to track in X posts. Any post containing these keywords will be monitored and included in reports. Use quotes for multi-word phrases. Keywords can include hashtags. Helps filter content by topics, brands, or specific terms relevant to your project.

Adds cashtags (token symbols) to track in X posts. Any post containing these cashtags will be monitored and included in reports. Cashtags must start with $ symbol. Useful for tracking cryptocurrency tokens, stock symbols, or project-specific ticker symbols.

Adds X handles to track when they are mentioned in posts. Any post that mentions these accounts (using @handle) will be monitored and included in reports. Different from add_accounts which tracks posts FROM accounts - this tracks posts ABOUT/MENTIONING accounts.

Adds keywords or phrases to exclude from tracking. Posts containing these excluded terms will be filtered out and NOT included in reports, even if they match other filter criteria. Use this to remove noise, spam, or irrelevant content from your tracking.

Adds X accounts to ignore. Posts from these accounts will be filtered out and NOT included in reports, even if they match other filter criteria. Use this to exclude bot accounts, spam accounts, or specific users whose posts you don't want to track.

Removes specific X accounts from tracking. Posts from these accounts will no longer be monitored or included in reports. Use this to stop tracking accounts that are no longer relevant to your project.
Removes specific keywords or phrases from tracking. Posts containing only these keywords will no longer be monitored. Use quotes for multi-word phrases.
Removes specific cashtags from tracking. Posts containing only these cashtags will no longer be monitored.
Removes X handles from mention tracking. Posts mentioning only these accounts will no longer be monitored.
Removes keywords from the exclusion list. Posts containing these terms will no longer be automatically filtered out.
Removes X accounts from the ignore list. Posts from these accounts will now be tracked if they match filter criteria.
Removes ALL tracked accounts from the filter. Use this to reset account tracking and start fresh. This does not delete the filter itself, only clears the accounts list.
Removes ALL keywords from the filter. Use this to reset keyword tracking.
Removes ALL cashtags from the filter. Use this to reset cashtag tracking.
Removes ALL mentions from the filter. Use this to reset mention tracking.
Removes ALL excluded keywords from the filter. Posts with previously excluded terms will now be tracked.
Removes ALL ignored accounts from the filter. Posts from previously ignored accounts will now be tracked.

pages.title: Leaderboard Customization | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documentation for X Bot leaderboard customization commands. Explains how administrators can configure point multipliers for engagement metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, views), set custom colors for visual reports, customize section titles, and control how many top users appear in generated leaderboards.

codeExamples.description: Set scoring multipliers for likes, retweets, replies, quotes, and views to calculate leaderboard rankings
Configure HTML hex colors for displaying engagement metrics in visual reports
Set custom title text and color for the main leaderboard section in reports

useCases: Customize engagement scoring to prioritize replies over likes for community building
Brand leaderboard reports with project-specific colors matching visual identity
Configure point weights to emphasize quality engagement (quotes) over passive metrics (views)
Set custom report titles for weekly competitions or campaign-specific tracking
Control leaderboard size to highlight top 10 or top 20 performers

Admin Only Copy /set_points 5 1 20 10 0.01 Parameters: Name Type Example likes required 50 retweets required 50 replies required 50 quotes required 50 views required value /get_points Retrieves and displays the current scoring multipliers for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) used in leaderboard calculations. Shows either configured values or indicates default values are being used. Copy /get_points /set_colors Sets the HTML colors for displaying engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) in the leaderboard reports. Colors are stored as hexadecimal values and used for report generation. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example likes required 50 retweets required 50 replies required 50 quotes required 50 views required value /get_colors Retrieves and displays the current HTML color configuration for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) used in leaderboard reports. Shows either configured colors or default values if none are set. /set_top_title Sets the title text and color for the main leaderboard section in generated reports. Allows customization of the primary heading displayed at the top of leaderboard reports. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example title_text required My Title color required #FF5733 /set_best_title Sets the title text and color for the best tweet section in generated reports. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example title_text required My Title color required #FF5733 /set_engagement_title Sets the title text and color for the engagement scoring section in generated reports. Allows customization of the heading displayed above engagement scoring rules and metrics. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example title_text required My Title color required #FF5733 /get_titles Retrieves and displays all current title configurations for the report sections including top title (main leaderboard), best title (best tweet), and engagement title (scoring rules). Shows either configured values or default values for each section. /set_top_count Sets the number of users to display in the report leaderboard. This controls how many top-performing users are shown in the generated reports. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example count required value /get_top_count Retrieves and displays the current number of users configured to show in the report leaderboard. Shows either the configured value or indicates default value (10) is being used. pages.title: Project Configuration | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Reference guide for X Bot project configuration commands. Covers setting project identity information including name, descriptions (short and long), logo, and URLs. These settings customize how the project appears in generated reports and on the public xbot.ninja dashboard for investor discovery.

codeExamples.description: Set the official project name that appears in all reports and on the website dashboard

codeExamples.code: /set_project_description A revolutionary DeFi platform for community governance
codeExamples.description: Set a short 1-2 sentence description of the project for reports
Add multiple project URLs including website and social media links for the dashboard

useCases: Brand analytics reports with official project name instead of Telegram group names
Provide project context to investors and partners viewing shared reports
Create a public dashboard page on xbot.ninja for investor discovery
Upload project logo to appear prominently in all generated reports
Add social media and documentation links for easy visitor access
pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions X Bot X Bot Commands Project Configuration /set_project_name Sets your project or community name that will appear in all generated reports, leaderboards, and on the website dashboard. This is the main identifier for your community and should be your project's official name or brand. Use this to customize reports with your project's identity instead of using generic Telegram group names. Admin Only Copy /set_project_name CryptoDAO Community Copy /set_project_name My Awesome Project Parameters: Name Type Example project_name required My Project /get_project_name Displays the current project name configured for your community. Use this to verify what name will appear in reports and on the website, or to check if a project name has been set yet. Admin Only Copy /get_project_name /set_project_description Sets a short description of your project that appears in reports and on the website. This should be a concise summary of what your project does or your community's mission (1-2 sentences). Use this to provide context about your project's goals when sharing reports with investors, partners, or community members. Admin Only Copy /set_project_description A revolutionary DeFi platform for community governance Parameters: Name Type Example description required value /get_project_description Displays the current project description configured for your community. Use this to review the short description that appears in reports, or to check if a description has been set. Admin Only /set_project_long_description Sets a detailed, comprehensive description of your project for the website dashboard. Unlike the short description, this can be multiple paragraphs and include more in-depth information about your project's mission, features, roadmap, and team. This appears on your public dashboard page to provide visitors with complete project details. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example long_description required value /get_project_long_description Displays the current detailed project description configured for the website dashboard. Use this to review the comprehensive description that visitors see on your public dashboard page, or to check if a long description has been set. Admin Only /set_project_logo Uploads your project logo that will appear on reports and the website dashboard. After running this command, the bot will prompt you to send an image file. Send your logo as a photo or file (PNG, JPG, or GIF recommended). The logo should be square or rectangular and will be displayed prominently in all generated reports and on your public dashboard page. Admin Only /get_project_logo Displays the URL of your currently configured project logo. Use this to verify which logo will appear in reports and on the website, or to share the logo URL with team members. If no logo is set, you'll receive a message prompting you to upload one with /set_project_logo. Admin Only /set_project_urls Sets important links for your project including website, social media, documentation, and other resources. These URLs appear on the website dashboard as clickable links, making it easy for visitors to connect with your project across different platforms. You can include your website, Twitter/X, Discord, Telegram, GitHub, documentation, or any other relevant links. Separate multiple URLs with spaces or commas. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example urls required https://myproject.com /get_project_urls Displays all project URLs currently configured for your community. Use this to review which links appear on the website dashboard, verify URLs are correct, or check if any URLs have been set. pages.title: Raid Commands | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Complete documentation for X Bot raid commands that enable coordinated community engagement campaigns on X (Twitter) posts. Covers launching raids with target metrics, tracking real-time progress, managing active raids, configuring defaults, and setting up automatic raids for top-performing content with customizable parameters.

codeExamples.description: Launch a coordinated raid on a specific X post with engagement targets and duration
Configure default settings for all future raids including targets and duration
Set up automatic raids that trigger for the best tweet of the day after reports

useCases: Amplify important project announcements by coordinating community engagement
Support partner posts with organized community raids
Drive visibility for product launches with targeted engagement campaigns
Automatically boost daily top-performing tweets to maximize reach
Track raid progress in real-time to monitor community participation
pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions X Bot X Bot Commands Raid Commands /raidx Launches a coordinated community raid on an X (Twitter) post to boost engagement. Community members receive a raid announcement with the target post and engagement goals (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks). The bot tracks progress in real-time and celebrates when targets are met. Raids help amplify important posts, support partners, or drive visibility for project announcements. Admin Only Copy /raidx https://x.com/username/status/123456 Copy /raidx https://x.com/username/status/123456 likes=50 retweets=20 Copy /raidx https://x.com/username/status/123456 duration=1h mute=yes Parameters: Name Type Example post_url required https://x.com/username/status/123456 likes optional 50 retweets optional 20 replies optional 50 quotes optional 50 bookmarks optional 50 duration optional 1h mute optional yes /raid_status Displays real-time progress of active raids, showing current engagement numbers vs. targets for likes, retweets, replies, quotes, and bookmarks. You can check a specific raid by ID or see the latest active raid. Use this to monitor how your community is progressing toward raid goals and motivate participation. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example raid_id optional value /raid_stop Cancels an active raid immediately, stopping progress tracking and notifications. Use this when you need to end a raid early - for example, if the target post was deleted, targets were already met naturally, or the raid was started by mistake. The raid status will be marked as "CANCELLED" in the raid history. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example raid_id required value /raid_history Displays a list of previous raids with their completion status, targets, and results. Review past raid performance to see which raids were successful, how long they took, and engagement levels achieved. Useful for analyzing community participation patterns and planning future raids. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example limit optional value /set_raid_defaults Configures default settings for all future raids including target engagement numbers, duration, auto-mute behavior, and whether raids are enabled. These defaults are used when you start a raid without specifying custom parameters. Set these once to match your community's typical raid goals, then override them per-raid when needed with /raidx parameters. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example likes optional 30 retweets optional 15 replies optional 10 quotes optional 5 bookmarks optional 20 duration optional 1h mute optional no enabled optional yes /get_raid_defaults Displays the current default raid settings including target numbers for all engagement types, default duration, auto-mute behavior, and raid enable/disable status. Use this to review your configured defaults before starting a raid or to verify settings after using /set_raid_defaults. Admin Only /set_raid_message_behavior Controls how the bot updates raid progress messages in the chat. Choose between three modes: "edit" (updates the existing message cleanly), "repost" (deletes and resends to keep it at the bottom of the chat), or "pin" (updates and pins the message to keep it visible at the top). Different modes suit different community preferences and chat activity levels. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example mode required value /get_raid_message_behavior Shows the currently configured raid message update mode (edit, repost, or pin) and explains what that mode does. Use this to verify how raid progress messages will be displayed in your chat. Admin Only /set_raid_image Uploads a custom image that appears in all raid announcement messages for your community. After running this command, the bot will prompt you to send an image file. This branded image helps make raid announcements more visually appealing and recognizable to your community. The image is displayed prominently in every raid message. Admin Only /auto_raid_config Configures automatic raids that trigger for the "Best Tweet of the Day" selected during daily report generation. When enabled, the bot automatically starts a raid on the top-performing post a few minutes after the report is published. This helps consistently boost your best content without manual raid creation. Configure raid duration, delay before starting, target engagement numbers, and auto-mute behavior. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example enabled optional no duration optional 360 delay optional 5 likes optional 100 retweets optional 50 replies optional 50 quotes optional 50 bookmarks optional 50 mute optional yes /get_auto_raid_config Displays the current auto-raid configuration including whether it's enabled, the raid duration, start delay after report, target engagement numbers, and auto-mute setting. Use this to review your auto-raid setup or verify changes after using /auto_raid_config. Admin Only Previous Project Configuration Next Schedule Management Last updated 4 days ago Was this helpful? /raidx /raid_status /raid_stop /raid_history /set_raid_defaults /get_raid_defaults /set_raid_message_behavior /get_raid_message_behavior /set_raid_image /auto_raid_config /get_auto_raid_config Was this helpful? Copy /raid_status Copy /raid_status RAID_123456 Copy /raid_stop RAID_123456 Copy /raid_history Copy /raid_history 20 Copy /set_raid_defaults likes=30 retweets=15 duration=1h Copy /set_raid_defaults enabled=yes mute=no duration=2h Copy /set_raid_defaults likes=50 retweets=25 replies=10 quotes=5 bookmarks=20 Copy /get_raid_defaults Copy /set_raid_message_behavior edit Copy /get_raid_message_behavior Copy /set_raid_image Copy /auto_raid_config enabled=no Copy /auto_raid_config enabled=yes duration=360 delay=5 Copy /auto_raid_config enabled=yes likes=100 retweets=50 duration=360 Copy /get_auto_raid_config

paragraphs: Launches a coordinated community raid on an X (Twitter) post to boost engagement. Community members receive a raid announcement with the target post and engagement goals (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks). The bot tracks progress in real-time and celebrates when targets are met. Raids help amplify important posts, support partners, or drive visibility for project announcements.

You can check a specific raid by ID or see the latest active raid. Use this to monitor how your community is progressing toward raid goals and motivate participation.

Cancels an active raid immediately, stopping progress tracking and notifications. Use this when you need to end a raid early - for example, if the target post was deleted, targets were already met naturally, or the raid was started by mistake. The raid status will be marked as "CANCELLED" in the raid history.

Displays a list of previous raids with their completion status, targets, and results. Review past raid performance to see which raids were successful, how long they took, and engagement levels achieved. Useful for analyzing community participation patterns and planning future raids.

These defaults are used when you start a raid without specifying custom parameters. Set these once to match your community's typical raid goals, then override them per-raid when needed with /raidx parameters.

Choose between three modes: "edit" (updates the existing message cleanly), "repost" (deletes and resends to keep it at the bottom of the chat), or "pin" (updates and pins the message to keep it visible at the top). Different modes suit different community preferences and chat activity levels.

Uploads a custom image that appears in all raid announcement messages for your community. After running this command, the bot will prompt you to send an image file. This branded image helps make raid announcements more visually appealing and recognizable to your community. The image is displayed prominently in every raid message.

Configures automatic raids that trigger for the "Best Tweet of the Day" selected during daily report generation. When enabled, the bot automatically starts a raid on the top-performing post a few minutes after the report is published. This helps consistently boost your best content without manual raid creation. pages.title: Reports | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documentation for X Bot report generation commands. Covers viewing the most recent leaderboard report and triggering immediate report recreation with fresh X (Twitter) data. Reports include visual charts, engagement statistics, and rankings based on configured scoring rules and can be accessed by any community member.

codeExamples.description: Display the most recently generated leaderboard report with visual charts and engagement statistics
Generate a fresh report immediately by fetching latest X data, bypassing scheduled generation
Create a new report and delete all existing report history to start fresh

useCases: Quickly share latest community performance data without waiting for scheduled reports
Generate updated reports after adding new tracked accounts or keywords
Create on-demand reports for presentations or investor meetings
Reset report history when starting a new tracking period or campaign
Provide community members instant access to engagement leaderboards

Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions X Bot X Bot Commands Reports /report Displays the most recently generated leaderboard report for your community. The bot sends both a visual chart image and formatted text showing engagement statistics and rankings for tracked accounts based on your configured scoring rules. Use this to quickly share the latest community performance data without waiting for the next scheduled report. Any community member can view reports - no admin privileges required. Admin Only Copy /report /recreate Triggers immediate generation of a fresh report by fetching latest X (Twitter) data and creating new leaderboards. This bypasses the normal scheduled report generation and consumes credits. Use this when you want an updated report outside the regular schedule, such as after adding new accounts to track or when preparing for a presentation. You can optionally delete all existing report history to start completely fresh. pages.title: Schedule Management | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Reference documentation for X Bot schedule management commands. Explains how to set up automated report generation using daily time scheduling or advanced cron expressions, retrieve current schedule settings, and delete configured schedules. Only administrators can manage reporting schedules.

codeExamples.description: Schedule daily reports at 12:30 UTC using simple time format
Schedule reports at 14:30 UTC only on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday
Use advanced cron expression for complex scheduling (weekdays at 12:00 UTC)

useCases: Automate daily analytics reports at peak community activity times
Schedule weekly leaderboard updates for community competitions
Configure weekday-only reports for business-focused projects
Set multiple daily report times for different timezone audiences
Create custom schedules using cron expressions for complex timing needs

Supports both simple daily time scheduling (HH:MM format) and advanced cron expressions for complex schedules. Creates an EventBridge rule for scheduled report generation. Only group administrators can execute this command. Shows the configured UTC time or indicates if no schedule is set. Copy /get_schedule /delete_schedule Removes the configured daily schedule for automatic report generation. Deletes the EventBridge rule and database entry. Only group administrators can execute this command. pages.title: Topic Management | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Reference guide for X Bot topic management commands in Telegram groups using forum-style topics. Explains how to configure which specific forum topic (thread) receives bot reports and updates, and how to verify the current topic destination for organized report management in forum-enabled groups.

codeExamples.description: Configure the current forum topic as the destination for all bot reports and updates
Display which forum topic is configured to receive bot reports showing name or ID

Verify report destination before scheduling automated reports

Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions X Bot X Bot Commands Topic Management /set_topic Configures which forum topic (thread) the bot should send reports to in Telegram groups that use forum-style topics. If your group uses Topics (Forums), run this command inside the specific topic where you want to receive bot reports and updates. The bot will automatically detect the current topic and save it as the destination for all future reports. Useful for keeping bot reports organized in a dedicated topic instead of cluttering the main chat. Admin Only Copy /set_topic /get_topic Displays which forum topic (thread) is currently configured to receive bot reports. Shows the topic name if available, or the thread ID if the name isn't stored. Use this to verify where the bot will send reports in forum-style groups, or to check if a topic has been configured at all. The setup process differs based on your use case — choose the path that matches your needs. Prerequisites Before installing X Bot, ensure you have: A Telegram account Admin access to a Telegram group (or ability to create one) An X (Twitter) account you want to track Basic familiarity with Telegram bots and group management Choose Your Installation Path Select the setup guide that matches your use case: Crypto Project Setup : Track your project's X engagement and community activity Individual KOL Setup : Monitor your personal X performance metrics KOL Team Setup : Track multiple team members' performance in one place Crypto Project Setup Goal: Track your project's X engagement and community activity (optionally use for competitions, prizes, or airdrops) 1 Find and Add the Bot Search for @BWS_X_Bot in Telegram Add the bot to your project's main Telegram group Make the bot an admin with the following settings: Admin rights required: Yes Special permissions needed: None 2 Create Your X Analytics Query The following is a simple example for first-time installation. You can customize tracking later. Track your official X account: Track your project's cashtag: Example: What this does: Tracks all posts from your project's X account Monitors posts mentioning your cashtag (e.g., $SOL ) Collects engagement metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks, impressions) Includes tracked items in leaderboard rankings 3 Schedule Daily Reports Set up automatic daily analytics reports: What this does: Generates automated reports daily at 12:00 UTC Posts performance screenshots and leaderboards to your group Tracks top accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and mentions Keeps community engaged with regular updates Tip: Choose a time when your community is most active for maximum engagement. 4 Verify Installation Force an initial report to verify everything is working: What this does: Creates an immediate performance report based on your configured tracking Shows leaderboards for accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and mentions Confirms tracking is working correctly Gives you a preview of daily automated reports 5 Add Project Metadata (Optional) Add project information to be featured on the xbot.ninja website: What this does: Displays your project on the xbot.ninja website for investor and community discovery Helps investors find projects with authentic community traction Showcases your project's performance metrics publicly 6 Customize Your Reports (Optional) Personalize reports with custom branding. Set Custom Point Weights (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Bookmarks, Impressions): Set Custom Colors: Set Custom Titles: Individual KOL Setup Goal: Get publicly listed on xbot.ninja to showcase your competencies and performance metrics. 1 Create Your Analytics Group Open Telegram and create a new group Name it something like "My X Analytics" or "KOL Performance Tracker" This can be a private group for personal tracking Tip: Keep this group private or share it with potential clients to showcase your metrics. 2 Find and Add X Bot Search for @BWS_X_Bot in Telegram Add the bot to your newly created group Make the bot an admin with: Admin rights required: Yes Special permissions needed: None 3 Track Your X Account Monitor your personal X account performance: Example: What this does: Tracks all your posts and engagement metrics Builds a verifiable portfolio of your performance Enables discovery on xbot.ninja Provides data for client reports 4 Add Your Topics Track keywords relevant to your content: What this does: Shows which topics you're covering Demonstrates your niche expertise Tracks community engagement with your topics Helps projects find you based on relevant keywords 5 Enable Regular Reports Get daily performance insights: What this does: Sends daily performance reports at 09:00 UTC Tracks engagement trends over time Helps identify top-performing content Provides data for client reporting 6 Create Your First Report Generate your first analytics report: What this does: Creates an immediate snapshot of your performance Shows engagement metrics and rankings Verifies tracking is working correctly Gives you shareable proof of your influence 7 Add Your Profile Metadata (Optional) Add profile information to be featured on the xbot.ninja website: What this does: Displays your KOL profile on xbot.ninja Helps project owners discover and hire you Showcases engagement metrics and expertise publicly To get publicly listed: Maintain a minimum performance threshold by posting and engaging consistently Build authentic engagement (avoid vanity metrics) Monitor daily reports to track progress toward listing Note: Featured KOLs are continuously monitored to maintain quality standards. KOL Team Setup Goal: Track team performance accurately and show clients real, measurable results 1 Create Team Analytics Group Open Telegram and create a new group Name it "KOL Team Analytics" or "[Team Name] Performance Tracker" This will be your shared analytics dashboard Tip: Make this group private and invite only team members and trusted clients. 2 Find and Add Bot as Admin Search for @BWS_X_Bot in Telegram Add the bot to your team group Make the bot an admin: Admin rights required: Yes Special permissions needed: None 3 Add Team Members Invite all KOL team members to the group: Click the group name to open group info Tap "Add Members" Select all team members Ensure everyone can see the analytics and reports 4 Track All Team Accounts Add all team members' X accounts for tracking: Example: What this does: Tracks team members' posts and engagement Creates team-wide leaderboards Enables performance comparisons Provides aggregate metrics for client reports 5 Set Projects You Work For Monitor client projects with cashtags: Example: What this does: Tracks team engagement with client tokens Shows which members promote which projects Demonstrates ROI to clients with verifiable metrics Identifies top performers per campaign 6 Schedule Team Reports Set up daily team performance reports: What this does: Generates daily reports at 10:00 UTC Posts leaderboards showing top team members Tracks trends for each project Provides regular updates for team and clients Tip: Schedule for team standups or planning meetings. 7 Create Your First Report Generate your first team analytics report: What this does: Creates an immediate team performance snapshot Shows individual and aggregate metrics Verifies all accounts are tracked correctly Provides shareable data for client presentations 8 Add Team Metadata (Optional) Feature your team on xbot.ninja : What this does: Displays your KOL team on xbot.ninja Helps project owners discover and hire your team Showcases team engagement metrics publicly 9 Customize Team Branding (Optional) Personalize reports with custom titles: 10 Exclude Internal Accounts (Optional) Hide internal/test accounts from leaderboards: Verification and Testing 1 Check bot status The bot should appear as an admin in your group 2 Test a command Run /help to see all available commands 3 Generate a report Run /recreate to create your first analytics report 4 Review the output Ensure accounts and keywords are tracked correctly 5 Check scheduled reports Wait for your scheduled time to verify automation Common Issues and Solutions Bot Not Responding Possible causes: Bot not made admin in the group Bot was removed or banned Network connectivity issues Solutions: Verify bot has admin status Remove and re-add the bot Check Telegram server status No Data in Reports Possible causes: Accounts or keywords not configured Tracking period too short (wait a few hours) X accounts have privacy restrictions Solutions: Run /get_accounts and /get_keywords to verify configuration Wait for X API to collect data (can take a few hours) Ensure tracked X accounts are public Reports Not Generating Possible causes: No schedule set Schedule time in wrong timezone (should be UTC) Insufficient data for report generation Solutions: Run /get_schedule to verify configuration Set schedule with /set_schedule HH:MM (UTC time) Manually generate report with /recreate to test Installation takes just a few minutes. Once configured, X Bot runs automatically, providing scheduled analytics to help you track, measure, and improve your X engagement. pages.summary: Overview documentation explaining X Bot as a Telegram-based analytics platform providing trusted X (Twitter) metrics for crypto communities. pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands Getting Support Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions X Bot X Bot Overview What is X Bot? X Bot is a Telegram-based analytics platform that provides trusted X (Twitter) analytics for crypto communities. It enables tracking, analysis, and reporting of X activity directly within Telegram groups. X analytics you can trust X Bot uses the official X API to ensure accuracy and reliability. We tested several cheaper alternatives and even built our own "posts crawler," but none matched the accuracy of the official API. Many third-party analytics tools you find online show up to 50% discrepancies in their metrics. With X Bot, you get data directly from the source you can trust. The Problem X Bot Solves In the crypto space, it's challenging to: Get a trusted measure of community engagement and the real impact of hired KOLs' profitability Identify projects with genuine community traction vs. artificial engagement Find and hire KOL teams that deliver actual results Track and measure authentic X (Twitter) engagement Verify influencer performance with transparent metrics Discover legitimate investment opportunities backed by real community activity X Bot solves these problems by providing verifiable X analytics that distinguish authentic engagement from vanity metrics. Key Features Analytics & Tracking Track X (Twitter) accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and mentions Monitor engagement metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks, impressions) Generate automated scheduled reports with performance insights (up to 12-hour frequency) View leaderboards ranking top performers by engagement Performance Scoring X Bot uses a scoring algorithm that weighs different engagement types based on their value. The weights are fully configurable to match each project's specific needs and priorities. Example scoring formula (weights can be customized): Example Engagement Weights Explained: Likes (×1): Basic engagement, shows content appreciation Retweets (×2): Amplification - user shares content with their followers Replies (×1.5): Active conversation starter Quotes (×3): High-value engagement - adds commentary while sharing Bookmarks (×2): Content worth saving for later reference Impressions (×0.001): Reach metric - how many people saw the post Example Calculation: For a post with: 50 likes, 10 retweets, 8 replies, 3 quotes, 15 bookmarks, 5,000 impressions Customization & Branding Custom point weights for different engagement types Branded colors for visual reports Custom titles for leaderboard sections Project metadata (name, logo, description, URLs) Named filters for different tracking scenarios Automated Reporting Schedule daily reports at specific times (UTC) Automatic performance screenshots Leaderboards showing top accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and mentions Historical tracking and trend analysis Dual-Purpose Analytics Platform X Bot serves multiple distinct but complementary audiences: For Project Owners Get a trusted measure of community engagement and hired KOLs' real profitability Measure your community's authentic X engagement and impact Track the real profitability and ROI of hired KOL campaigns Discover KOL teams with proven performance metrics Hire influencers who deliver actual engagement and results Monitor KOL performance with transparent, verifiable data Avoid influencers with fake followers or inflated metrics What you get: Trusted measurement of community engagement levels Real performance data on KOL teams and their ROI Comparative metrics across multiple influencers Evidence of authentic community building Data-driven decisions for influencer partnerships For KOLs and KOL Teams Showcase your competencies and get discovered Build a public portfolio of verifiable performance metrics Get discovered by serious projects looking for proven performers Track team performance accurately to show clients measurable results Stand out from competitors with transparent data Demonstrate real ROI to potential clients For Investors Discover crypto projects with authentic community traction Visit xbot.ninja to explore our new investment discovery feature: Find projects with genuine X activity and real engagement Identify legitimate investment opportunities backed by actual community activity Analyze projects based on verifiable metrics, not vanity numbers Make data-driven investment decisions What to look for: Consistent engagement across multiple metrics Growing community participation Authentic conversations and discussions Real influencer involvement How Projects and KOLs Get Featured X Bot curates high-quality data from projects and KOL teams through a selection process. 1 X Bot Installation Must have successfully installed and configured X Bot in their Telegram community. Demonstrates commitment to transparent analytics. 2 Minimum Performance Threshold Achieve a minimum performance score indicating sustained X activity, showing genuine engagement over time. 3 Active Community Engagement Consistent posting and meaningful interactions, authentic community growth patterns, and regular engagement from community members. 4 Quality Content Standards High-quality X presence with genuine engagement metrics, no artificial inflation or bot-driven activity, and authentic interactions and conversations. Why Curation Matters The curation process ensures quality for all stakeholders: Investors: Find legitimate projects with real community backing Project Owners: Discover KOL teams that deliver actual engagement and ROI KOLs: Get discovered by serious projects looking for proven performers Everyone: Access transparent, verifiable metrics reflecting authentic activity All projects and KOL teams are continuously monitored to maintain quality standards. Use Cases Crypto Projects Boost community X engagement with competitions and prizes Track your project's official X account Monitor community hashtags and cashtags Run engagement competitions with transparent leaderboards Reward top performers with prizes Build authentic community participation Individual KOLs Get publicly listed to showcase your competencies Create a personal analytics tracking group Monitor your X account performance Build a verifiable portfolio of engagement metrics Get discovered by projects seeking influencers Prove your value with transparent data KOL Teams Track team performance and show clients real results Set up shared analytics for your entire team Track all team members' X accounts in one place Monitor project hashtags you're promoting Generate team performance reports Demonstrate ROI to clients with verifiable metrics Benefits Summary Transparency All metrics are verifiable and sourced directly from X (Twitter) No manipulation or artificial inflation Real-time data updates Authenticity Performance scoring weighs meaningful engagement over vanity metrics Curated selection ensures quality participants Continuous monitoring maintains standards Actionable Insights Daily automated reports with actionable data Leaderboards identifying top performers Historical trends and performance tracking Customizable views for different analysis needs Community Building Gamification through leaderboards and competitions Recognition for top contributors Customizable views for different analysis needs Previous X Bot Next X Bot Installation Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? Likes (×1): Basic engagement, shows content appreciation
Retweets (×2): Amplification - user shares content with their followers
Replies (×1.5): Active conversation starter
Quotes (×3): High-value engagement - adds commentary while sharing
Bookmarks (×2): Content worth saving for later reference
Impressions (×0.001): Reach metric - how many people saw the post

Product Blurb Product Blurb Target: 100-200 words | Actual: 160 words, 1091 chars Copy X Bot is your trusted solution for tracking and analyzing X (Twitter) engagement. Using the official X API, it provides accurate metrics to help crypto projects measure their community impact, enables KOL teams to showcase verifiable performance, and allows investors to discover projects with authentic traction. With X Bot, you can track specific accounts, monitor keywords and hashtags, follow cryptocurrency cashtags, see who's mentioning important accounts, and filter out noise. The flexible filtering system supports complex setups through named filters. You'll get automated daily reports with leaderboards ranking top performers by engagement. Customize the scoring, visual appearance, and branding to match your needs. X Bot serves multiple audiences: - Projects can measure community engagement and hired KOLs' real ROI - KOLs and teams can build public portfolios of verified metrics to get discovered - Investors can find legitimate projects backed by genuine community activity Install X Bot in your Telegram group in minutes and start tracking your performance today! 🚀📈 Target Audiences Crypto Projects - Crypto projects seeking to boost authentic community engagement on X (Twitter) and track performance metrics. pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees Fees Calculator API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Platform Fees We take care of everything. Calling blockchain smart contracts requires funds: “It is the fuel that allows it to operate”. Fees compensate miners for their computational efforts in processing and securing transactions on the network. They are fundamental to the blockchain's sustainability, security, and efficiency, ensuring the network remains robust and decentralized. Blockchain Web Services (BWS) aims to transform the blockchain market by making innovative solutions accessible to a broad audience, including those without technical or Web3 experience. Fees Calculator Interacting with blockchain requires paying fees, so it's important to understand how Blockchain Web Services manages those fees to offer our customers the best user experience. In any scenario, we handle all the blockchain complexities. You interact with the blockchain(s) we support using our simplified API, and you don't need to worry about anything other than naming the blockchain you want to use. If you haven't entered your credit card, you can test our blockchain solutions for free using the Mumbai or Sepolia test networks. You can run 100 blockchain transactions for free. We use our token pools to pay network fees, and you are not charged. Using test networks does not consume $BWS tokens. Once you move into subscription mode by entering your credit card details, You can run 1000 testnet transactions for free. When running on main networks, we do real-time trading, convert US dollars to the required network token, and pay the fees for you. You will then be charged for those fees on your next bill, using 0.01 USD as the minimum billable unit. Every solution requires $BWS tokens to execute and fund the developer's community and platform maintenance. Please read the platform White Paper to know more about the $BWS token and its tokenomics. Pricing Example Suppose you subscribe to Blockchain Web Services and want to create 100 new NFTs on the Polygon blockchain using BWS.NFT.zK to create the NFTs, and BWS.IPFS.Upload to save your images to IPFS. Creating those 100 NFTs will cost you: Your next bill will show an extra 2 USD to your monthly plan. How do I pay those Fees? To check your current monthly cost, go to the Billing menu and check your credit usage. BLOCKCHAIN CREDIT FEE We use the Blockchain Credit Fee as the minimum billable unit, 0.01 USD. If, for example, you spend 2 USD creating NFTs, we will add 200 blockchain credit fee units to your next bill. Credits Usage balance. pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Quick Start Writing "Hello World!" to the blockchain in just one call! Get your API key Your API requests should be authenticated using your API key (any request that doesn't include an API key will return an error). Learn how to Get your API Key . Your First BWS API Request We're going to use the BWS.Blockchain.Hash solution to save a message to the blockchain. Write "Hello World!" to Matchain blockchain. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/smart-contracts/call Let's write "Hello World!" to the Matchain blockchain network without managing network fees or Web3 wallets by calling a regular Web2 API. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.Blockchain.Hash version * number 1 network * string matchain operation * string insert parameters * JSON { key: "mykey", value: "Hello World!" } Please copy & Paste one of the following code snippets and run it (remember to use your API Key). curl Javascript Phyton If executed correctly, check for jobId in the response body part. We will use it to verify that the blockchain operation has been completed correctly and to receive the blockchain transaction receipt and Certificate of Trust . API Call response Check for Status To check for job completion and results, use the jobId you got from the previous call. Please note that interacting with blockchain(s) is asynchronous, so it may take some time for the operation to finish and for the blockchain receipt to be received. Fetch for the blockchain job status and results. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/smart-contracts/fetch Using the previous call response jobId we fetch the blockchain job started previously. Request Body Name Type Description jobId * String (use previously returned jobId, as for example 59d0f46b-0b58-4972-8aca-51d06f69f25e) Copy & Paste one of the following code snippets and run it (remember to use your own API Key). curl Javascript Phyton You should receive a response indicating the completed job and the related blockchain receipt and certificate. Fetch API Call Response Congratulations! In just one call, you used BWS to save data into the Web3 blockchain ecosystem. Previous Welcome! Next Platform Fees Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? Get your API key Your First BWS API Request Write "Hello World!" to Matchain blockchain. Check for Status Fetch for the blockchain job status and results. Was this helpful? Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/smart-contracts/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.Blockchain.Hash", "version": 1, "network": "matchain", "operation": "insert", "parameters": { "key": "timestamp: 1736418591440", "value": "Hola" } }' Copy var settings = { "url": "https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call", "method": "POST", "timeout": 0, "headers": { "X-Api-Key": "API-KEY", "Content-Type": "application/json" }, "data": JSON.stringify({ "solution": "BWS.Blockchain.Hash", "version": 1, "network": "matchain", "operation": "insert", "parameters": { "key": "timestamp: 1736418591440", "value": "Hola" } }), }; $.ajax(settings).done(function (response) { console.log(response); }); Copy import http.client import json conn = http.client.HTTPSConnection("api.bws.ninja") payload = json.dumps({ "solution": "BWS.Blockchain.Hash", "version": 1, "network": "matchain", "operation": "insert", "parameters": { "key": "timestamp: 1736418591440", "value": "Hola" } }) headers = { 'X-Api-Key': 'API-KEY', 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } conn.request("POST", "/v1/call", payload, headers) res = conn.getresponse() data = res.read() print(data.decode("utf-8")) Copy { "statusCode": 200, "statusMessage": "", "info": { "jobId": "59d0f46b-0b58-4972-8aca-51d06f69f25e" } } Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/fetch' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "jobId": "59d0f46b-0b58-4972-8aca-51d06f69f25e" }' Copy var settings = { "url": "https://api.bws.ninja/v1/fetch", "method": "POST", "timeout": 0, "headers": { "X-Api-Key": "API-KEY", "Content-Type": "application/json" }, "data": JSON.stringify({ "jobId": "59d0f46b-0b58-4512-8aca-51d06f69f25e" }), }; $.ajax(settings).done(function (response) { console.log(response); }); Copy import http.client import json conn = http.client.HTTPSConnection("api.bws.ninja") payload = json.dumps({ "jobId": "59d0f46b-0b58-4512-8aca-51d06f69f25e" }) headers = { 'X-Api-Key': 'API-KEY', 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } conn.request("POST", "/v1/fetch", payload, headers) res = conn.getresponse() data = res.read() print(data.decode("utf-8")) Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": { "status": { "Value": "completed" }, "request": { "solution": "BWS.Blockchain.Hash", "version": 1, "network": "matchain", "operation": "insert", "parameters": { "key": "timestamp: 1736418591440", "value": "Hola" } }, "hash": "0xd5c51886e57b1b16e59bd866910f2c689728f9e4025a4abc14bf7b07ae21dcc8", "txnUrl": "https://matchscan.io/tx/0xd5c51886e57b1b16e59bd866910f2c689728f9e4025a4abc14bf7b07ae21dcc8", "certificateOfTrust": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/bws-backoffice-website-infra-prod/tx/matchain/0xd5c51886e57b1b16e59bd866910f2c689728f9e4025a4abc14bf7b07ae21dcc8.pdf", "certificateOfTrustJPG": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/bws-backoffice-website-infra-prod/tx/matchain/0xd5c51886e57b1b16e59bd866910f2c689728f9e4025a4abc14bf7b07ae21dcc8.jpg", "certificateOfTrustUrl": "https://prod.bws.ninja/tx.html?t=0xd5c51886e57b1b16e59bd866910f2c689728f9e4025a4abc14bf7b07ae21dcc8", "type": { "value": "blockchain-job" }, "timestampInMillis": 1736418605359, "id": "a89bf8bf-392b-4cc3-8ded-7dbe9d43c341" } }

pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Solution overview for BWS.Blockchain.Hash explaining hash database concepts and blockchain-based key-value storage. Demonstrates using unique client IDs or transaction numbers as hash keys for quick data retrieval. Emphasizes blockchain immutability for data integrity and includes Certificate of Trust for each entry.
useCases: Manage client data with unique customer IDs as hash keys
Store transaction records for quick retrieval and auditing
Ensure data integrity using blockchain's immutable nature
Provide clients with Certificate of Trust for stored data
Implement rapid data access for reporting and verification
pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash Solution Overview Operations BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy PLATFORM APIs BWS.Blockchain.Hash Solution Overview Use the blockchain to efficiently store data in blockchain(s). BWS.Blockchain.Hash simplifies saving data to the blockchain, enabling you to use it as a hash database while abstracting the complexities of blockchain infrastructure. What is a Hash Database? A hash database is a type of database that uses hash keys to efficiently retrieve and store data. Each record is assigned a unique key, often derived from the data or predefined attributes, which acts as an identifier for quick lookups. Hash databases are known for their speed and scalability, making them ideal for applications requiring rapid data access. An example: saving client data to the blockchain. Businesses can use BWS.Blockchain.Hash to manage client data by storing essential information (e.g., customer profiles or transaction records) in a blockchain-based hash database. The system uses unique keys, such as client IDs or transaction numbers, to retrieve data effortlessly. Here's how this could work: Client Information Use a unique client ID as the hash key to link to stored client details or references. Data Retrieval Quickly access data associated with a hash key for reporting, auditing, or verification purposes. Enhanced Trust Leverage blockchain’s immutable nature to ensure the integrity and transparency of stored records. Why Choose BWS.Blockchain.Hash? This solution is perfect for individuals and businesses that want to take advantage of blockchain as a trusted and efficient hash-based storage system. With BWS.Blockchain.Hash , you can store and retrieve data securely, gain the benefits of blockchain transparency, and provide clients with a Certificate of Trust for each entry—ensuring their data is handled responsibly and verifiably. Available Networks We're currently updating this solution smart contracts for Polygon (main) and Mumbai (test). Please use Matchain. Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload Solution Overview Operations BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload Operations Easily upload Images, PDF and JSON files to IPFS. Upload to IPFS Use this operation to upload content to IPFS and get the IPFS Content Identifier (CID). POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.IPFS.Upload operation * string new parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the IPFS CID and useful URIs. Copy { "statusCode" : 200 , "info" : { "cid" : "QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" , "uris" : { "ipfs" : "ipfs://QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" , "url" : "https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" } } } Upload to IPFS Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption description string Short description of your content (will be returned when listing your IPFS files ). content JSON / base64 Content to save on the IPFS network. We currently support: JSON (just pass the JSON or the stringified string) File sent as a base64 encoded string Please note that when uploading your file: You must optimize your content to be less than 1 MB in size (there is a hard limit of 5MB when uploading files to IPFS using our API). We currently support JSON, images, and PDF files. If your use case requires higher file sizes or other file types, please contact us. Upload to IPFS Examples Upload an Image using nodejs Upload JSON using cURL Upload to IPFS call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the IPFS Content Identifier (CID) along with the corresponding IPFS URI and URL. These details facilitate easy access and reference to the uploaded content on the IPFS network. List IPFS Files Get the list of your IPFS files POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.IPFS.Upload operation * string list parameters JSON check Method Parameters 200 The list of files you uploaded to IPFS List IPFS Files Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption from long Unix time in milliseconds representing a timestamp to select IPFS files from. to long Unix time in milliseconds representing a timestamp to select IPFS files to. LIST IPFS Files Request Example cURL List IPFS Files call Response Once executed correctly, you will get a list of the IPFS uploaded files using BWS API. That list will contain for each file: the timestamp of creation ( Unix time in milliseconds), the IPFS Content Identifier (CID), the file type, and the URIs to easily access the file IPFS URI, web URL - using the BWS IPFS Gateway to fetch the file.
