Copy const fs = require('fs'); const axios = require('axios'); /* get the file you want to upload to IPFS */ const fileData = fs.readFileSync('./files/image.png'); /* encode file content to base64 */ const encodedData = fileData.toString('base64'); /* build request to use BWS IPFS solution */ const request = { "solution": "BWS.IPFS.Upload", "operation": "new", "parameters": { description: "My first IPFS file using BWS API!", parameters: { content: encodedData } } }; /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method: 'post', maxBodyLength: Infinity, url: 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call', headers: { 'X-Api-Key': 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v', /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, data : JSON.stringify(request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console.log(JSON.stringify(response.data)); }) .catch((error) => { console.log(error); }); Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: XqaLg...729v' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.IPFS.Upload", "operation": "new", "parameters": { "description": "Just a simple JSON for testing BWS API", "content": { "text": "Hello World!" } } }' Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": { "cid": "QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN", "uris": { "ipfs": "ipfs://QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN", "url": "https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" } } } Copy { "statusCode": 200, "statusMessage": "", "info": [ { "": "" } ] } Copy curl --location 'https://api.staging.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: XqaLg...729v' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.IPFS.Upload", "operation": "list" "parameters": { "from": 1700404140000 "to": 1700411940000 } }' Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": [ { "timestamp": "1700416540067", "cid": "QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN", "description": "Just a simple JSON for testing BWS API", "fileType": "json", "uris": { "ipfs": "ipfs://QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN", "url": "https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" } }, { "timestamp": "1700121600009", "cid": "QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowkiu", "description": "image for token 1325", "fileType": "image", "uris": { "ipfs": "ipfs://QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowkiu", "url": "https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowkiu" } } ] }

pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: BWS IPFS API Service leverages the InterPlanetary File System for decentralized storage of images, PDF, and JSON files. Files are distributed across the network with unique content identifiers (CID) and accessible via the ipfs.bws.ninja gateway, providing immutable, tamper-proof storage for NFT metadata, decentralized content delivery, and data integrity verification.
useCases: Store NFT metadata JSON files and associated images for blockchain applications
Host static resources like logos or graphics for web or app projects
Share structured data in a transparent, verifiable way for data integrity and provenance
Decentralized content delivery for permanent file storage
Upload and retrieve images, PDF and JSON files via IPFS gateway
pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload Solution Overview Operations BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload Solution Overview Easily upload Images, PDF and JSON files to IPFS. BWS IPFS API Service leverages the InterPlanetary File System (IPFS) , a distributed, peer-to-peer storage protocol designed to create a permanent and decentralized method of storing and sharing data. IPFS breaks files into smaller chunks, distributes them across the network, and assigns each file a unique content identifier (CID) for efficient retrieval. BWS IPFS Gateway Use ipfs.bws.ninja as the gateway to access the files you have uploaded to IPFS using BWS. An IPFS gateway acts as a bridge between the decentralized IPFS network and traditional web browsers, allowing users to access content stored on IPFS using standard HTTP protocols. This makes IPFS content easily accessible without requiring specialized software or configuration. Check out our lovely unicorn on IPFS using the BWS Gateway: https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmU7avmnTb4iVbNGCHMJmdEbsx9nRtUx7dWGpKeE7zjo8T Key Features of IPFS Decentralized Storage Files are stored across multiple nodes, enhancing reliability and fault tolerance. Content Addressing Data is retrieved using its CID, ensuring that files are immutable and tamper-proof. Global Accessibility IPFS data can be accessed by any node in the network, fostering collaboration and resilience. Supported File Types Our service supports uploading images. PDF and JSON files , making it ideal for use cases where these formats are predominant. Examples include: NFT Metadata and Assets Storing JSON metadata files and associated images for blockchain applications. Decentralized Content Delivery Hosting static resources like logos or graphics for web or app projects. Data Integrity and Provenance Sharing structured data (e.g., JSON) in a transparent, verifiable way. Previous BWS.IPFS.Upload Next Operations Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? BWS IPFS Gateway Key Features of IPFS Supported File Types Was this helpful?

pages.title: NFT Attributes (traits) | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: NFT attributes (traits) define unique characteristics of non-fungible tokens through metadata. Each trait consists of a trait_type (category like 'Fur Color' or 'Rarity') and a value (specific characteristic like 'Black' or 'Ultra-Rare'), enabling NFT uniqueness and influencing perceived value based on trait rarity.

codeExamples.description: Example NFT metadata structure showing how traits define unique characteristics for an animated cat NFT collection

useCases: Create unique digital art collections with distinct features using trait attributes
Define rarity levels for NFTs through specific trait combinations
Structure NFT metadata with categorical traits for collectibles
Visualize NFT characteristics in Certificate of Trust for user experience
Influence NFT perceived value through rare trait combinations
pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Solution Overview Operations NFT Attributes (traits) Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy PLATFORM APIs BWS.NFT.zK NFT Attributes (traits) Making your NFT unique. Non-fungible tokens (NFTs) represent unique digital assets on blockchain platforms. Those assets can define associated traits , which are specific attributes or characteristics that detail each NFT. In a practical scenario, an NFT's metadata might look something like this for a digital art collection of animated cats: Copy { "name" : "Animated Cat #001" , "description" : "A unique animated cat with distinct features." , "image" : "ipfs://QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowkiu" , "attributes" : [ { "trait_type" : "Fur Color" , "value" : "Black" } , { "trait_type" : "Eye Type" , "value" : "Round" } , { "trait_type" : "Accessories" , "value" : "Hat" } ] } Within the metadata, the attributes array contains the various traits of the NFT. Each trait is defined by a trait_type , which specifies the category of the trait (e.g., "Fur Color" or "Rarity"), and a value , which indicates the specific characteristic of that trait (e.g., "Black" or "Ultra-Rare"). Traits encapsulated within this metadata not only bring out the uniqueness of each token but can also influence its perceived value, especially if some traits are considered rarer than others. BWS.NFT.zK allows you to create an NFT by passing those traits . Please note that it's the usage of those traits when visualizing your NFT that you must take care of and validate. For example, if you use the provided Certificate of Trust for your users' experience use case, you must check they are correctly displayed. Previous Operations Next Blockchain Badges Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? Was this helpful?

pages.title: Operations | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Complete API reference for BWS.NFT.zK operations including Create NFT (mint new NFTs with metadata and attributes), List NFTs (retrieve NFT collections), Transfer NFT (change ownership to wallet address), and Send NFT by Email (transfer via email with owner/receiver codes). All operations are asynchronous with jobId tracking.

codeExamples.description: Create a new NFT on Mumbai network with name, description, image URL, and rarity attribute
Response from Create NFT operation returning jobId for tracking transaction status

useCases: Mint new NFTs on blockchain networks with custom metadata and attributes
List and retrieve NFT collections with date range filtering
Transfer NFT ownership to blockchain wallet addresses
Send NFTs via email to recipients without Web3 wallets
Track NFT creation and transfer status using asynchronous job IDs

implementationSteps.title: Create NFT
implementationSteps.description: Call the API with solution 'BWS.NFT.zK', operation 'new', and parameters including name, description, image URL/IPFS URI, and optional attributes. Receive a jobId in response.

implementationSteps.title: Check Transaction Status
implementationSteps.description: Use the fetch API with the provided jobId to monitor blockchain transaction confirmation and retrieve NFT details including nftId, transaction hash, and IPFS metadata hash.

implementationSteps.title: Transfer Ownership
implementationSteps.description: Use the 'transfer' operation with nftId and wallet address, or 'send' operation with nftId and email address. For email transfers, provide owner and receiver codes to complete the transfer.

Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Solution Overview Operations Create NFT List NFTs Transfer NFT Send NFT by Email NFT Attributes (traits) Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy PLATFORM APIs BWS.NFT.zK Operations BWS.NFT.zK API Operations. Create NFT This operation is asynchronous (the blockchain network takes a while to confirm transactions). Check the blockchain transaction status and receipt using the fetch API and the provided jobId. Creates a new NFT. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to create (Mint) a new NFT on a selected blockchain Network. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK version * number 1 network * string check available networks operation * string new parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the related job Id Copy { "statusCode" : 200 , "statusMessage" : "" , "info" : { "jobId" : "59d0f46b-0b58-4972-8aca-51d06f69f25e" } } Create NFT Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption name string Name of your NFT. description string The NFT description. image URL (or IPFS URI) This is the URL or IPFS URI of your NFT image. attributes optional JSON These are the attributes of your NFT. Create NFT Request Example cURL Create NFT Fetch Response Create NFT Fetch Response Example The fetch operation , once the NFT creation job is completed, will return the following: Parameter Type Description guid string NFT unique id network string Network the NFT has been created in. nftTxHash string Blockchain transaction hash nftId string NFT id nftIPFSHash string NFT metadata file IPFS hash nftImageIPFSHash string NFT image file IPFS hash nftJson json NFT metadata json url json (check NFT Urls ) status string The NFT status: "created" or "transferred" transferCodes json (check Transfer Codes ) NFT Urls Easy-to-use URLs for you to get access to NFT's most relevant data. Parameter Type Description nft URL NFT metada URL image URL NFT image URL transaction URL NFT blockchain transaction explorer URL Transfer Codes Transfer codes that are required to transfer ownership when sending an NFT through email (check NFT Ownership to learn more). Parameter Type Description owner string Owner code is required to execute a transfer when sending an NFT over email. receiver string The receiver code the new owner should indicate to execute the NFT transfer. List NFTs Lists your NFTs. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Get the list of your NFTs. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK operation * string list parameters JSON check Methods Parameters 200 Returns the list of your NFTs. List NFT Method Parameters If no range is defined, the last 20 NFTs are returned. Parameter Type Desciption from long Number of milliseconds representing a timestamp to select NFTs from. to long Number of milliseconds representing a timestamp to select NFTs to. List IPFS Files call Response LIST NFT Request Example cURL List NFT Response The list operation returns the list of your NFTs, including NFT-relevant data and the network you created the NFT in. List NFT Fetch Response Example Transfer NFT This operation is asynchronous. (once executed, check the results using fetch API and the provided jobId) Transfer an NFT ownership. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Transfer an NFT ownership by using the new owner's wallet or email address. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK version * number 1 network * string check available networks operation * string transfer parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the related job Id Transfer NFT Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption nftId string The unique identifier of the NFT to be transferred. address string Wallet address to send the NFT to (check NFT Owerneship ) Transfer NFT Request Example cURL Transfer NFT Fetch Response The fetch operation for the NFT transfer API call will mainly state if the transfer has been successful ( completed ) and the related costs. Transfer NFT Fetch Response Example Send NFT by Email Send an NFT by email. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Send an NFT by using the new owner's email address. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK network * string check available networks operation * string send parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the related job Id Send NFT Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption nftId string The unique identifier of the NFT to be transferred. email string Email address to send the NFT to (check NFT Owerneship ) Send NFT Request Example cURL Send NFT by Email Response As a confirmation message response, if the NFT send is confirmed you will get your NFT-related details, including the codes that the new owner will have to use to execute a blockchain NFT transfer. paragraphs: BWS.NFT.zK API Operations.
Use this operation to create (Mint) a new NFT on a selected blockchain Network.
This is the URL or IPFS URI of your NFT image.
These are the attributes of your NFT.
Network the NFT has been created in.
Blockchain transaction hash
NFT metadata file IPFS hash
NFT image file IPFS hash
The NFT status: "created" or "transferred"
NFT blockchain transaction explorer URL
Owner code is required to execute a transfer when sending an NFT over email.
The receiver code the new owner should indicate to execute the NFT transfer.
Get the list of your NFTs.
If no range is defined, the last 20 NFTs are returned.
Number of milliseconds representing a timestamp to select NFTs from.
Number of milliseconds representing a timestamp to select NFTs to.
The list operation returns the list of your NFTs, including NFT-relevant data and the network you created the NFT in.
Transfer an NFT ownership by using the new owner's wallet or email address.
The unique identifier of the NFT to be transferred.
Send an NFT by using the new owner's email address.
The unique identifier of the NFT to be transferred.
As a confirmation message response, if the NFT send is confirmed you will get your NFT-related details, including the codes that the new owner will have to use to execute a blockchain NFT transfer.

pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: BWS.NFT.zK solution overview explaining NFT ownership models and data storage. NFTs are initially linked to BWS accounts with two transfer options: wallet address or email. Images and metadata are always stored on IPFS, supporting multiple blockchain networks including Matchain, Polygon, and Ethereum.

codeExamples.description: API call JSON example showing NFT creation with URL-based image reference and rarity attribute
NFT metadata example with IPFS URI for image storage, showing final metadata structure stored on IPFS

useCases: Transfer NFT ownership to blockchain wallet addresses for Web3-enabled users
Send NFTs via email to recipients without Web3 wallets using owner/receiver codes
Maintain NFT custody in BWS account before transferring ownership
Store NFT images and metadata permanently on IPFS with automatic uploading
Create NFTs on multiple blockchain networks including Matchain, Polygon, and Ethereum

implementationSteps.title: Create NFT with BWS Custody
implementationSteps.description: Create a new NFT using BWS.NFT.zK which initially links to your BWS account. Provide image as URL or IPFS URI; images are automatically uploaded to IPFS if needed.

implementationSteps.title: Transfer to Wallet Address
implementationSteps.description: Use Transfer NFT operation to transfer ownership directly to a blockchain wallet address for users with Web3 wallets.

implementationSteps.title: Transfer via Email
implementationSteps.description: Use Send NFT by Email operation for recipients without Web3 wallets. The new owner receives email notification and can later transfer to their own wallet using provided owner/receiver codes.

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Solution Overview NFT Ownership NFT Data Location Available Networks Operations NFT Attributes (traits) Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy PLATFORM APIs BWS.NFT.zK Solution Overview Easily create NFTs. NFT Ownership When you create a new NFT using BWS.NFT.zK (Zero Knowledge) solution, the NFT will initially be linked to your BWS account. In order to transfer an NFT ownership, you have two options: Transfer ownership to a blockchain wallet address. Transfer ownership to an email address. Before you transfer your NFT, we will do the custody, and ownership is mapped with your BWS Account. Transfer NFT Ownership to a Wallet If you want to transfer an NFT ownership to another wallet address, you can simply use the Transfer NFT operation indicating the new blockchain wallet address owner. Transfer NFT Ownership to an Email Address If you want to transfer an NFT ownership, but the new owner does not have a Web3 Wallet (or you don't know it), you can simply transfer the ownership by using his email address. Email received for the new NFT owner to hold and get ownership We will send the new owner an email notification indicating he's the owner of the NFT. If he wants to transfer the ownership to his own Wallet, he will be able to. Transfer ownership page for wasy NFT transfers Please note that once you transfer the ownership using an email address, ONLY the email address owner will be able to transfer the NFT to a new Wallet address. NFT Metadata and Image Location NFT Image Your NFT image is always hosted in IPFS. To create an NFT using BWS.NFT.zK, you pass the NFT image as: a web addressable URL, or, as an IPFS URI (for example, ipfs://QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowki). API Call JSON Example using a URL for the NFT image. In all the scenarios, your NFT metadata will point to an image stored in IPFS, as we will upload your image to IPFS if required. NFT Metadata Your NFT metadata is always hosted in IPFS. When a new NFT is created, we create and upload the NFT metadata to IPFS, NFT Metadata Example Your NFT blockchain transaction then points to IPFS, as you can see on the following example: NFT Creation Transaction Parameters Networks We're updating this solution smart contracts for Polygon and Ethereum networks. Please use Matchain. We currently offer NFT creation in Polygon and Ethereum blockchains. Blockchain Network Id Description Matchain matchain Matchain blockchain. Polygon polygon Polygon blockchain. mumbai Polygon test network. Ethereum ethereum Ethereum blockchain We recommend using the Polygon Mumbai network for tests. Smart Contracts We're updating this solution smart contracts for Polygon and Ethereum networks. Please use Matchain. Click on Contract Address to check the verified contract. paragraphs: When you create a new NFT using BWS.NFT.zK (Zero Knowledge) solution, the NFT will initially be linked to your BWS account. In order to transfer an NFT ownership, you have two options:
Transfer ownership to a blockchain wallet address.
Transfer ownership to an email address.
Before you transfer your NFT, we will do the custody, and ownership is mapped with your BWS Account.
If you want to transfer an NFT ownership, but the new owner does not have a Web3 Wallet (or you don't know it), you can simply transfer the ownership by using his email address.
We will send the new owner an email notification indicating he's the owner of the NFT. If he wants to transfer the ownership to his own Wallet, he will be able to.
Please note that once you transfer the ownership using an email address, ONLY the email address owner will be able to transfer the NFT to a new Wallet address.
To create an NFT using BWS.NFT.zK, you pass the NFT image as:
a web addressable URL, or,
as an IPFS URI (for example, ipfs://QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowki).
API Call JSON Example using a URL for the NFT image.
In all the scenarios, your NFT metadata will point to an image stored in IPFS, as we will upload your image to IPFS if required.
When a new NFT is created, we create and upload the NFT metadata to IPFS,
